/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba src/cli/commands/room.ts | sed -n '1,260p'" in /Users/saharbarak/personal/akashik
 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	/**
     2	 * `akashik room <sub>` — manage the room registry.
     3	 *
     4	 * Subcommands:
     5	 *   list            show all rooms + which is default
     6	 *   create <name>   create a room (prompts or flags for details)
     7	 *   switch <id>     set the default room
     8	 *   current         print the current default room
     9	 *   describe <id>   show full metadata for a room
    10	 *
    11	 * Example:
    12	 *   akashik room create homelab --desc "Home lab infra" --keywords "proxmox,mikrotik,10gbe"
    13	 *   akashik room switch homelab
    14	 */
    15	
    16	import { formatError } from '../../domain/errors.js';
    17	import type { RoomMeta } from '../../domain/rooms.js';
    18	import { slugifyRoomName } from '../../domain/rooms.js';
    19	import { fileRoomsConfig } from '../../infrastructure/rooms-config.js';
    20	import { runtimePaths } from '../runtime.js';
    21	import { join } from 'node:path';
    22	
    23	const roomsPath = (): string => join(runtimePaths().home, 'rooms.json');
    24	
    25	// ─────────────── subcommands ──────────────
    26	
    27	const list = async (): Promise<number> => {
    28	  const cfg = fileRoomsConfig(roomsPath());
    29	  const result = await cfg.load();
    30	  if (result.isErr()) {
    31	    console.error(`room list: ${formatError(result.error)}`);
    32	    return 1;
    33	  }
    34	  const registry = result.value;
    35	  if (registry.rooms.length === 0) {
    36	    console.log('no rooms configured. try `akashik init` or `akashik room create <name>`.');
    37	    return 0;
    38	  }
    39	  for (const r of registry.rooms) {
    40	    const marker = r.id === registry.default_room ? ' *' : '  ';
    41	    const keywords = r.keywords.length > 0 ? ` [${r.keywords.join(', ')}]` : '';
    42	    console.log(`${marker} ${r.id.padEnd(20)} ${r.description}${keywords}`);
    43	  }
    44	  console.log(`\n  * = default room`);
    45	  return 0;
    46	};
    47	
    48	const create = async (rest: readonly string[]): Promise<number> => {
    49	  if (rest.length === 0) {
    50	    console.error('room create: missing <name>. usage: akashik room create <name> [--desc "..."] [--keywords "a,b,c"] [--wing "default"]');
    51	    return 1;
    52	  }
    53	  const name = rest[0];
    54	  const id = slugifyRoomName(name);
    55	  let description = '';
    56	  let keywords: string[] = [];
    57	  let wing: string | undefined;
    58	
    59	  for (let i = 1; i < rest.length; i++) {
    60	    const a = rest[i];
    61	    const next = (): string | undefined => rest[++i];
    62	    if (a === '--desc' || a === '--description') description = next() ?? '';
    63	    else if (a.startsWith('--desc=')) description = a.slice('--desc='.length);
    64	    else if (a === '--keywords') keywords = (next() ?? '').split(',').map((k) => k.trim()).filter(Boolean);
    65	    else if (a.startsWith('--keywords=')) keywords = a.slice('--keywords='.length).split(',').map((k) => k.trim()).filter(Boolean);
    66	    else if (a === '--wing') wing = next();
    67	    else if (a.startsWith('--wing=')) wing = a.slice('--wing='.length);
    68	  }
    69	
    70	  const room: RoomMeta = {
    71	    id,
    72	    name,
    73	    description: description || `Research room for ${name}`,
    74	    keywords,
    75	    default_wing: wing,
    76	    created_at: new Date().toISOString(),
    77	  };
    78	
    79	  const cfg = fileRoomsConfig(roomsPath());
    80	  const result = await cfg.create(room);
    81	  if (result.isErr()) {
    82	    console.error(`room create: ${formatError(result.error)}`);
    83	    return 1;
    84	  }
    85	  console.log(`created room '${id}' (${room.description})`);
    86	  if (result.value.default_room === id) {
    87	    console.log(`  set as default room`);
    88	  }
    89	  return 0;
    90	};
    91	
    92	const switchRoom = async (rest: readonly string[]): Promise<number> => {
    93	  const id = rest[0];
    94	  if (!id) {
    95	    console.error('room switch: missing <id>');
    96	    return 1;
    97	  }
    98	  const cfg = fileRoomsConfig(roomsPath());
    99	  const result = await cfg.setDefault(id);
   100	  if (result.isErr()) {
   101	    console.error(`room switch: ${formatError(result.error)}`);
   102	    return 1;
   103	  }
   104	  console.log(`default room is now '${id}'`);
   105	  return 0;
   106	};
   107	
   108	const current = async (): Promise<number> => {
   109	  const cfg = fileRoomsConfig(roomsPath());
   110	  const result = await cfg.load();
   111	  if (result.isErr()) {
   112	    console.error(formatError(result.error));
   113	    return 1;
   114	  }
   115	  const def = result.value.default_room;
   116	  if (!def) {
   117	    console.log('no default room set. run `akashik init` to create one.');
   118	    return 0;
   119	  }
   120	  console.log(def);
   121	  return 0;
   122	};
   123	
   124	const describe = async (rest: readonly string[]): Promise<number> => {
   125	  const id = rest[0];
   126	  if (!id) {
   127	    console.error('room describe: missing <id>');
   128	    return 1;
   129	  }
   130	  const cfg = fileRoomsConfig(roomsPath());
   131	  const result = await cfg.load();
   132	  if (result.isErr()) {
   133	    console.error(formatError(result.error));
   134	    return 1;
   135	  }
   136	  const room = result.value.rooms.find((r) => r.id === id);
   137	  if (!room) {
   138	    console.error(`room describe: room '${id}' not found`);
   139	    return 1;
   140	  }
   141	  console.log(`id:           ${room.id}`);
   142	  console.log(`name:         ${room.name}`);
   143	  console.log(`description:  ${room.description}`);
   144	  console.log(`keywords:     ${room.keywords.join(', ') || '(none)'}`);
   145	  console.log(`default wing: ${room.default_wing ?? '(none)'}`);
   146	  console.log(`created:      ${room.created_at}`);
   147	  console.log(`is default:   ${result.value.default_room === room.id ? 'yes' : 'no'}`);
   148	  return 0;
   149	};
   150	
   151	// ─────────────── entry ──────────────────
   152	
   153	export const room = async (args: readonly string[]): Promise<number> => {
   154	  const [sub, ...rest] = args;
   155	  switch (sub) {
   156	    case undefined:
   157	    case 'list':
   158	      return list();
   159	    case 'create':
   160	      return create(rest);
   161	    case 'switch':
   162	      return switchRoom(rest);
   163	    case 'current':
   164	      return current();
   165	    case 'describe':
   166	      return describe(rest);
   167	    default:
   168	      console.error(`room: unknown subcommand '${sub}'. try: list | create | switch | current | describe`);
   169	      return 1;
   170	  }
   171	};

/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba src/domain/sharing.ts | sed -n '1,210p'" in /Users/saharbarak/personal/akashik
 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	/**
     2	 * Pure domain functions for the sharing security boundary.
     3	 *
     4	 * `scanNode` projects a GraphNode → ShareableNode while checking every
     5	 * scannable field against the secrets pattern set. A node that matches
     6	 * any pattern is hard-blocked: the function returns err(ScanError)
     7	 * with full match details — callers cannot override this (SEC-02).
     8	 *
     9	 * `auditRoom` applies `scanNode` across a list of nodes and partitions
    10	 * the result into allowed / blocked buckets (used by `share audit` CLI).
    11	 *
    12	 * `buildPatterns` composes the 10 built-in patterns with any extra
    13	 * patterns supplied via config.yaml (SecurityConfig.secrets_patterns).
    14	 *
    15	 * Design constraints
    16	 * ------------------
    17	 * - ShareableNode deliberately excludes `file_type` and `source_file`
    18	 *   (SEC-03: metadata boundary — those fields reveal local filesystem
    19	 *   paths and internal classification that peers must not receive).
    20	 * - Every regex uses the 'g' flag. CRITICAL: re.lastIndex MUST be reset
    21	 *   to 0 before each `.test()` call, otherwise stateful global regexes
    22	 *   will produce false-negatives on consecutive calls.
    23	 * - No I/O, no classes, no throws — pure functions throughout.
    24	 */
    25	
    26	import { Result, err, ok } from 'neverthrow';
    27	import type { GraphNode } from './graph.js';
    28	import type { ScanError, ScanMatch } from './errors.js';
    29	import { ScanError as SE } from './errors.js';
    30	
    31	// ─────────────────────── types ────────────────────────────
    32	
    33	/**
    34	 * The subset of GraphNode fields that are safe to share with peers.
    35	 *
    36	 * Intentionally excludes:
    37	 *   - `file_type`   (SEC-03: reveals internal classification)
    38	 *   - `source_file` (SEC-03: reveals local filesystem paths)
    39	 *   - `embedding_vector` (raw float arrays) — see SEC-03 design note below
    40	 *   - raw text / content fields (always excluded at this boundary)
    41	 *
    42	 * SEC-03 design note — why embedding_id, not embedding_vector:
    43	 * The project requirements originally listed "embedding vector" as shareable.
    44	 * Phase 15 ships `embedding_id` (a reference) instead of the raw float array
    45	 * after review: embedding-inversion attacks can recover approximate source
    46	 * text from sentence-transformer vectors (see Morris et al. 2023, "Text
    47	 * Embeddings Reveal Almost As Much As Text"). Sharing the vector would make
    48	 * the metadata boundary porous. Cross-peer semantic search (Phase 17) must
    49	 * re-embed from source_uri + label on the receiving side, not trust imported
    50	 * vectors. REQUIREMENTS.md SEC-03 has been updated to reflect this choice.
    51	 */
    52	export interface ShareableNode {
    53	  readonly id: string;
    54	  readonly label: string;
    55	  readonly room: string;
    56	  readonly embedding_id?: string;
    57	  readonly source_uri?: string;
    58	  readonly fetched_at?: string;
    59	}
    60	
    61	export interface AuditResult {
    62	  readonly allowed: readonly ShareableNode[];
    63	  readonly blocked: ReadonlyArray<{
    64	    readonly nodeId: string;
    65	    readonly matches: readonly ScanMatch[];
    66	  }>;
    67	}
    68	
    69	// ─────────────────────── patterns ─────────────────────────
    70	
    71	/**
    72	 * 14 built-in secret patterns. All use the 'g' flag — callers MUST
    73	 * reset re.lastIndex = 0 before each test() invocation.
    74	 *
    75	 * Pattern hardening notes:
    76	 *   - bearer-token now anchors to JWT shape (ey[JK]... . ... . ...)
    77	 *     to avoid flagging research notes that merely mention "Bearer tokens"
    78	 *   - private-key-block covers RSA/EC/OPENSSH/PGP/ENCRYPTED/DSA headers
    79	 *   - google-api-key, slack-token, github-pat-fine added per review feedback
    80	 */
    81	const BUILT_IN_PATTERNS: ReadonlyArray<{ readonly name: string; readonly re: RegExp }> =
    82	  Object.freeze([
    83	    { name: 'openai-key',        re: /sk-[a-zA-Z0-9]{20,}/g },
    84	    { name: 'github-token',      re: /ghp_[a-zA-Z0-9]{36}/g },
    85	    { name: 'github-oauth',      re: /gho_[a-zA-Z0-9]{36}/g },
    86	    { name: 'github-pat-fine',   re: /github_pat_[A-Za-z0-9_]{82}/g },
    87	    { name: 'aws-key-id',        re: /AKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}/g },
    88	    { name: 'stripe-live',       re: /sk_live_[a-zA-Z0-9]{24}/g },
    89	    { name: 'bearer-token',      re: /Bearer\s+ey[JK][A-Za-z0-9_-]{20,}\.[A-Za-z0-9_-]+\.[A-Za-z0-9_-]+/g },
    90	    { name: 'google-api-key',    re: /AIza[0-9A-Za-z_-]{35}/g },
    91	    { name: 'slack-token',       re: /xox[baprs]-[A-Za-z0-9-]{10,}/g },
    92	    { name: 'private-key-block', re: /-----BEGIN (RSA |EC |OPENSSH |PGP |ENCRYPTED |DSA )?PRIVATE KEY-----/g },
    93	    { name: 'password-kv',       re: /password\s*[=:]\s*\S{6,}/gi },
    94	    { name: 'api-key-kv',        re: /api[_-]?key\s*[=:]\s*["']?[a-zA-Z0-9._-]{10,}/gi },
    95	    { name: 'env-token',         re: /[A-Z_]{3,}_TOKEN=[^\s"']{8,}/g },
    96	    { name: 'env-secret',        re: /[A-Z_]{3,}_SECRET=[^\s"']{8,}/g },
    97	  ]);
    98	
    99	/**
   100	 * Fields of ShareableNode that are scanned for secrets.
   101	 * Includes id/room/embedding_id defensively — those fields are rare
   102	 * sources of leaks but cheap to scan, and a user-derived id could
   103	 * accidentally contain token-shaped content.
   104	 */
   105	const SCANNABLE_FIELDS: ReadonlyArray<keyof ShareableNode> = [
   106	  'id',
   107	  'label',
   108	  'room',
   109	  'source_uri',
   110	  'fetched_at',
   111	  'embedding_id',
   112	];
   113	
   114	// ─────────────────────── public API ───────────────────────
   115	
   116	/**
   117	 * Compose the full pattern set from built-ins plus any extras.
   118	 * Extras come from `SecurityConfig.secrets_patterns` in config.yaml.
   119	 * Returns a new array — BUILT_IN_PATTERNS is never mutated.
   120	 */
   121	export const buildPatterns = (
   122	  extras: ReadonlyArray<{ readonly name: string; readonly pattern: string }> = [],
   123	): ReadonlyArray<{ readonly name: string; readonly re: RegExp }> => [
   124	  ...BUILT_IN_PATTERNS,
   125	  ...extras.map(({ name, pattern }) => ({ name, re: new RegExp(pattern, 'g') })),
   126	];
   127	
   128	/**
   129	 * Project a GraphNode to ShareableNode, scanning every string field
   130	 * against the supplied patterns.
   131	 *
   132	 * Returns ok(ShareableNode) if no secrets detected.
   133	 * Returns err(ScanError) if any pattern matches — the node is hard-blocked.
   134	 */
   135	export const scanNode = (
   136	  node: GraphNode,
   137	  patterns: ReturnType<typeof buildPatterns>,
   138	): Result<ShareableNode, ScanError> => {
   139	  const shareable: ShareableNode = {
   140	    id: node.id,
   141	    label: node.label,
   142	    room: node.room ?? '',
   143	    embedding_id: node.embedding_id,
   144	    source_uri: node.source_uri,
   145	    fetched_at: node.fetched_at,
   146	  };
   147	
   148	  const matches: ScanMatch[] = [];
   149	
   150	  for (const field of SCANNABLE_FIELDS) {
   151	    const value = shareable[field];
   152	    if (typeof value !== 'string') continue;
   153	
   154	    for (const { name, re } of patterns) {
   155	      // CRITICAL: reset lastIndex before every test() call.
   156	      // Global regexes are stateful — forgetting this causes false-negatives
   157	      // when the same regex object is reused across multiple test() calls.
   158	      re.lastIndex = 0;
   159	      if (re.test(value)) matches.push({ field, patternName: name });
   160	    }
   161	  }
   162	
   163	  return matches.length > 0
   164	    ? err(SE.secretDetected(node.id, matches))
   165	    : ok(shareable);
   166	};
   167	
   168	/**
   169	 * Audit an entire room's worth of nodes for sharing safety.
   170	 * Partitions nodes into allowed (clean) and blocked (flagged) buckets.
   171	 * Preserves order — allowed nodes maintain their original sequence.
   172	 */
   173	export const auditRoom = (
   174	  nodes: readonly GraphNode[],
   175	  patterns: ReturnType<typeof buildPatterns>,
   176	): AuditResult => {
   177	  const allowed: ShareableNode[] = [];
   178	  const blocked: Array<{ nodeId: string; matches: ScanMatch[] }> = [];
   179	
   180	  for (const node of nodes) {
   181	    const result = scanNode(node, patterns);
   182	    if (result.isOk()) {
   183	      allowed.push(result.value);
   184	    } else {
   185	      blocked.push({ nodeId: node.id, matches: [...result.error.matches] });
   186	    }
   187	  }
   188	
   189	  return { allowed, blocked };
   190	};

 succeeded in 0ms:
   120	  g: Graph,
   121	  relation: string,
   122	  source: NodeId,
   123	): readonly GraphEdge[] => g.edgesByRelSource.get(relEdgeKey(relation, source)) ?? [];
   124	
   125	/** Search result for traversal queries. */
   126	export interface Subgraph {
   127	  readonly nodes: readonly GraphNode[];
   128	  readonly edges: readonly GraphEdge[];
   129	}
   130	
   131	/** Options for traversal queries. */
   132	export interface TraversalOptions {
   133	  readonly depth?: number;
   134	  /** if present, only nodes in this room are eligible. */
   135	  readonly room?: Room;
   136	}
   137	
   138	// ─────────────────────── constants ────────────────────────
   139	
   140	const REQUIRED_NODE_FIELDS = ['id', 'label', 'file_type', 'source_file'] as const;
   141	const REQUIRED_EDGE_FIELDS = ['source', 'target', 'relation', 'confidence', 'source_file'] as const;
   142	
   143	const EMPTY_JSON: GraphJson = {
   144	  directed: false,
   145	  multigraph: false,
   146	  graph: { hyperedges: [] },
   147	  nodes: [],
   148	  links: [],
   149	};
   150	
   151	// ─────────────────────── construction ─────────────────────
   152	
   153	/** An empty graph. */
   154	export const empty = (): Graph => fromJsonUnchecked(EMPTY_JSON);
   155	
   156	/**
   157	 * Parse raw JSON into a validated Graph. Rejects malformed input at
   158	 * the boundary. Accepts both `links` (graphify's canonical) and
   159	 * `edges` (for compatibility with older NetworkX JSON dumps).
   160	 */
   161	export const fromJson = (raw: unknown, path = '<memory>'): Result<Graph, GraphError> => {
   162	  if (!raw || typeof raw !== 'object') {
   163	    return err(GraphError.parseError(path, 'graph root must be an object'));
   164	  }
   165	  const obj = raw as Record<string, unknown>;
   166	  const nodes = Array.isArray(obj.nodes) ? (obj.nodes as GraphNode[]) : [];
   167	  const links: readonly GraphEdge[] = Array.isArray(obj.links)
   168	    ? (obj.links as GraphEdge[])
   169	    : Array.isArray(obj.edges)
   170	      ? (obj.edges as GraphEdge[])
   171	      : [];
   172	  const graph = (typeof obj.graph === 'object' && obj.graph !== null
   173	    ? (obj.graph as GraphJson['graph'])
   174	    : { hyperedges: [] }) as GraphJson['graph'];
   175	
   176	  // Validate every node. We're strict at the boundary so downstream
   177	  // domain ops can assume every node has the required fields.
   178	  for (const n of nodes) {
   179	    for (const f of REQUIRED_NODE_FIELDS) {
   180	      if (!n[f]) return err(GraphError.invalidNode(f, n.id));
   181	    }
   182	  }
   183	  for (const e of links) {
   184	    for (const f of REQUIRED_EDGE_FIELDS) {
   185	      if (!e[f]) return err(GraphError.invalidEdge(f));
   186	    }
   187	  }
   188	
   189	  const json: GraphJson = {
   190	    directed: Boolean(obj.directed),
   191	    multigraph: Boolean(obj.multigraph),
   192	    graph,
   193	    nodes,
   194	    links,
   195	  };
   196	  return ok(fromJsonUnchecked(json));
   197	};
   198	
   199	/** Serialize a Graph to the NetworkX node-link JSON shape. */
   200	export const toJson = (g: Graph): GraphJson => g.json;
   201	
   202	// ─────────────────────── queries ──────────────────────────
   203	
   204	export const size = (g: Graph): { nodes: number; edges: number } => ({
   205	  nodes: g.json.nodes.length,
   206	  edges: g.json.links.length,
   207	});
   208	
   209	export const getNode = (g: Graph, id: NodeId): GraphNode | undefined => g.nodeById.get(id);
   210	
   211	export const hasNode = (g: Graph, id: NodeId): boolean => g.nodeById.has(id);
   212	
   213	export const nodesInRoom = (g: Graph, room: Room): readonly GraphNode[] =>
   214	  g.json.nodes.filter((n) => n.room === room);
   215	
   216	export const neighbors = (g: Graph, id: NodeId): readonly GraphNode[] => {
   217	  const adj = g.adjacency.get(id);
   218	  if (!adj) return [];
   219	  const out: GraphNode[] = [];
   220	  for (const nid of adj) {
   221	    const n = g.nodeById.get(nid);
   222	    if (n) out.push(n);
   223	  }
   224	  return out;
   225	};
   226	
   227	// ─────────────────────── traversal ────────────────────────
   228	
   229	/** Breadth-first traversal from a set of starting nodes. */
   230	export const bfs = (g: Graph, start: readonly NodeId[], opts: TraversalOptions = {}): Subgraph => {
   231	  const depth = opts.depth ?? 3;
   232	  const allow = roomFilter(g, opts.room);
   233	  const visited = new Set<NodeId>();
   234	  const frontier: NodeId[] = [];
   235	  for (const s of start) {
   236	    if (g.nodeById.has(s) && allow(s)) {
   237	      visited.add(s);
   238	      frontier.push(s);
   239	    }
   240	  }
   241	  let current = frontier;
   242	  for (let d = 0; d < depth; d++) {
   243	    const next: NodeId[] = [];
   244	    for (const nid of current) {
   245	      const adj = g.adjacency.get(nid);

/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba src/domain/graph.ts | sed -n '245,455p'" in /Users/saharbarak/personal/akashik

   245	      const adj = g.adjacency.get(nid);
   246	      if (!adj) continue;
   247	      for (const m of adj) {
   248	        if (visited.has(m) || !allow(m)) continue;
   249	        visited.add(m);
   250	        next.push(m);
   251	      }
   252	    }
   253	    if (next.length === 0) break;
   254	    current = next;
   255	  }
   256	  return subgraph(g, visited);
   257	};
   258	
   259	/** Depth-first traversal from a set of starting nodes. */
   260	export const dfs = (g: Graph, start: readonly NodeId[], opts: TraversalOptions = {}): Subgraph => {
   261	  const depth = opts.depth ?? 3;
   262	  const allow = roomFilter(g, opts.room);
   263	  const visited = new Set<NodeId>();
   264	  const stack: Array<[NodeId, number]> = [];
   265	  for (let i = start.length - 1; i >= 0; i--) {
   266	    const s = start[i];
   267	    if (g.nodeById.has(s) && allow(s)) stack.push([s, 0]);
   268	  }
   269	  while (stack.length > 0) {
   270	    const [nid, d] = stack.pop()!;
   271	    if (visited.has(nid) || d > depth) continue;
   272	    visited.add(nid);
   273	    const adj = g.adjacency.get(nid);
   274	    if (!adj) continue;
   275	    for (const m of adj) {
   276	      if (visited.has(m) || !allow(m)) continue;
   277	      stack.push([m, d + 1]);
   278	    }
   279	  }
   280	  return subgraph(g, visited);
   281	};
   282	
   283	/** Unweighted shortest path (BFS). Returns an empty array if no path. */
   284	export const shortestPath = (
   285	  g: Graph,
   286	  source: NodeId,
   287	  target: NodeId,
   288	  maxHops = 8,
   289	): readonly NodeId[] => {
   290	  if (!g.nodeById.has(source) || !g.nodeById.has(target)) return [];
   291	  if (source === target) return [source];
   292	  const prev = new Map<NodeId, NodeId>();
   293	  const visited = new Set<NodeId>([source]);
   294	  const queue: NodeId[] = [source];
   295	  let hops = 0;
   296	  while (queue.length > 0 && hops <= maxHops) {
   297	    const layerSize = queue.length;
   298	    for (let i = 0; i < layerSize; i++) {
   299	      const nid = queue.shift()!;
   300	      const adj = g.adjacency.get(nid);
   301	      if (!adj) continue;
   302	      for (const m of adj) {
   303	        if (visited.has(m)) continue;
   304	        visited.add(m);
   305	        prev.set(m, nid);
   306	        if (m === target) return reconstruct(prev, source, target);
   307	        queue.push(m);
   308	      }
   309	    }
   310	    hops++;
   311	  }
   312	  return [];
   313	};
   314	
   315	// ─────────────────────── mutators (pure) ──────────────────
   316	
   317	/**
   318	 * Insert or update a node. Returns a new graph with the node applied.
   319	 * Existing attributes are merged shallowly — pass only the fields you
   320	 * want to change.
   321	 */
   322	export const upsertNode = (g: Graph, node: GraphNode): Result<Graph, GraphError> => {
   323	  for (const f of REQUIRED_NODE_FIELDS) {
   324	    if (!node[f]) return err(GraphError.invalidNode(f, node.id));
   325	  }
   326	  const existing = g.nodeById.get(node.id);
   327	  const merged: GraphNode = existing ? { ...existing, ...node } : { ...node };
   328	  const nodes = existing
   329	    ? g.json.nodes.map((n) => (n.id === node.id ? merged : n))
   330	    : [...g.json.nodes, merged];
   331	  return ok(fromJsonUnchecked({ ...g.json, nodes }));
   332	};
   333	
   334	/**
   335	 * Wholesale replace a node — discard ALL existing attributes, write
   336	 * exactly the provided shape.
   337	 *
   338	 * Use this for entity stubs (codex review M5 on batch-ingest.ts:267):
   339	 * canonical entity fields (`aliases`, `mention_count`, `note`) live
   340	 * in `entities.json`, not the graph. A previous schema (or a third-
   341	 * party tool that wrote the graph file) might have left those fields
   342	 * on a stub node — `upsertNode`'s shallow merge would preserve them
   343	 * forever even after entities.json moved on. `replaceNode` enforces
   344	 * "graph stub IS the canonical projection, nothing else."
   345	 */
   346	export const replaceNode = (g: Graph, node: GraphNode): Result<Graph, GraphError> => {
   347	  for (const f of REQUIRED_NODE_FIELDS) {
   348	    if (!node[f]) return err(GraphError.invalidNode(f, node.id));
   349	  }
   350	  const existed = g.nodeById.has(node.id);
   351	  const replacement: GraphNode = { ...node };
   352	  const nodes = existed
   353	    ? g.json.nodes.map((n) => (n.id === node.id ? replacement : n))
   354	    : [...g.json.nodes, replacement];
   355	  return ok(fromJsonUnchecked({ ...g.json, nodes }));
   356	};
   357	
   358	/**
   359	 * Insert or update an edge. Both endpoints must already exist. Edges
   360	 * are undirected — the same pair (a,b) and (b,a) refer to the same
   361	 * edge, keyed by the sorted pair.
   362	 */
   363	export const upsertEdge = (g: Graph, edge: GraphEdge): Result<Graph, GraphError> => {
   364	  for (const f of REQUIRED_EDGE_FIELDS) {
   365	    if (!edge[f]) return err(GraphError.invalidEdge(f));
   366	  }
   367	  if (!g.nodeById.has(edge.source)) return err(GraphError.danglingEdge(edge.source, edge.target));
   368	  if (!g.nodeById.has(edge.target)) return err(GraphError.danglingEdge(edge.source, edge.target));
   369	
   370	  const key = edgeKey(edge.source, edge.target);
   371	  const existingIdx = g.json.links.findIndex((e) => edgeKey(e.source, e.target) === key);
   372	  if (existingIdx >= 0) {
   373	    const merged: GraphEdge = { ...g.json.links[existingIdx], ...edge };
   374	    const links = g.json.links.map((e, i) => (i === existingIdx ? merged : e));
   375	    return ok(fromJsonUnchecked({ ...g.json, links }));
   376	  }
   377	  return ok(fromJsonUnchecked({ ...g.json, links: [...g.json.links, edge] }));
   378	};
   379	
   380	/** Remove a node and all edges incident to it. */
   381	export const removeNode = (g: Graph, id: NodeId): Result<Graph, GraphError> => {
   382	  if (!g.nodeById.has(id)) return err(GraphError.nodeNotFound(id));
   383	  const nodes = g.json.nodes.filter((n) => n.id !== id);
   384	  const links = g.json.links.filter((e) => e.source !== id && e.target !== id);
   385	  return ok(fromJsonUnchecked({ ...g.json, nodes, links }));
   386	};
   387	
   388	// ─────────────────────── internals ────────────────────────
   389	
   390	/** Build the indexed Graph view from a JSON envelope. Private. */
   391	const fromJsonUnchecked = (json: GraphJson): Graph => {
   392	  const nodeById = new Map<NodeId, GraphNode>();
   393	  const adjacency = new Map<NodeId, Set<NodeId>>();
   394	  for (const n of json.nodes) {
   395	    nodeById.set(n.id, n);
   396	    if (!adjacency.has(n.id)) adjacency.set(n.id, new Set());
   397	  }
   398	  // Inbound + outbound edge indices keyed by `${relation}${id}`.
   399	  // Built in the same loop that updates adjacency to keep load
   400	  // cost O(edges). Memory: ~50 bytes per edge × edge count.
   401	  const edgesByRelTarget = new Map<string, GraphEdge[]>();
   402	  const edgesByRelSource = new Map<string, GraphEdge[]>();
   403	  for (const e of json.links) {
   404	    const a = adjacency.get(e.source) ?? new Set<NodeId>();
   405	    const b = adjacency.get(e.target) ?? new Set<NodeId>();
   406	    a.add(e.target);
   407	    b.add(e.source);
   408	    adjacency.set(e.source, a);
   409	    adjacency.set(e.target, b);
   410	
   411	    const tk = relEdgeKey(e.relation, e.target);
   412	    const sk = relEdgeKey(e.relation, e.source);
   413	    const tArr = edgesByRelTarget.get(tk);
   414	    if (tArr) tArr.push(e);
   415	    else edgesByRelTarget.set(tk, [e]);
   416	    const sArr = edgesByRelSource.get(sk);
   417	    if (sArr) sArr.push(e);
   418	    else edgesByRelSource.set(sk, [e]);
   419	  }
   420	  // Freeze to make mutation-via-cast impossible.
   421	  return Object.freeze({
   422	    json,
   423	    nodeById,
   424	    adjacency,
   425	    edgesByRelTarget,
   426	    edgesByRelSource,
   427	  });
   428	};
   429	
   430	const roomFilter =
   431	  (g: Graph, room: Room | undefined): ((id: NodeId) => boolean) =>
   432	  (id) => {
   433	    if (!room) return true;
   434	    return g.nodeById.get(id)?.room === room;
   435	  };
   436	
   437	const subgraph = (g: Graph, nodeIds: ReadonlySet<NodeId>): Subgraph => {
   438	  const nodes: GraphNode[] = [];
   439	  for (const id of nodeIds) {
   440	    const n = g.nodeById.get(id);
   441	    if (n) nodes.push(n);
   442	  }
   443	  const edges = g.json.links.filter((e) => nodeIds.has(e.source) && nodeIds.has(e.target));
   444	  return { nodes, edges };
   445	};
   446	
   447	const edgeKey = (a: NodeId, b: NodeId): string => (a < b ? `${a}|${b}` : `${b}|${a}`);
   448	
   449	const reconstruct = (
   450	  prev: Map<NodeId, NodeId>,
   451	  source: NodeId,
   452	  target: NodeId,
   453	): readonly NodeId[] => {
   454	  const path: NodeId[] = [target];
   455	  let cur = target;

/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba src/domain/graph-lint.ts | sed -n '1,190p'" in /Users/saharbarak/personal/akashik
 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	/**
     2	 * Graph lint — pure rules that surface hygiene issues in the graph.
     3	 *
     4	 * Port of claude-obsidian's wiki-lint, adapted for our graph model
     5	 * (node IDs + edges + rooms, not wiki pages + wikilinks). Also adds
     6	 * P2P-specific checks that claude-obsidian doesn't need.
     7	 *
     8	 * Every rule is a pure function:  (Graph, opts?) -> Findings[]
     9	 * Rules don't mutate, don't fix — fixes happen in the application
    10	 * layer against the GraphRepository so the domain stays I/O-free.
    11	 *
    12	 * Categories:
    13	 *   L1 orphan            — node with no incident edges
    14	 *   L2 dead-edge         — edge whose source or target node is gone
    15	 *   L3 missing-room      — node with no room assigned
    16	 *   L4 missing-fetched-at — node without fetched_at (unknown age)
    17	 *   L5 empty-label       — node with blank / whitespace-only label
    18	 *   L6 duplicate-uri     — two or more nodes share source_uri (dedupe opportunity)
    19	 *   L7 unshared-p2p      — node stamped peer:/p2p: that lives in a room NOT marked shared
    20	 *   L8 stale-secret-match — node content matches a current secret pattern (drift audit)
    21	 */
    22	
    23	import type { Graph, GraphNode } from './graph.js';
    24	import { size as graphSize, nodesInRoom } from './graph.js';
    25	import type { buildPatterns } from './sharing.js';
    26	import { redactNode } from './secret-gate.js';
    27	
    28	export type LintCategory =
    29	  | 'orphan'
    30	  | 'dead-edge'
    31	  | 'missing-room'
    32	  | 'missing-fetched-at'
    33	  | 'empty-label'
    34	  | 'duplicate-uri'
    35	  | 'unshared-p2p'
    36	  | 'stale-secret-match';
    37	
    38	export interface Finding {
    39	  readonly category: LintCategory;
    40	  readonly node_id?: string;
    41	  readonly room?: string;
    42	  readonly detail: string;
    43	}
    44	
    45	export interface LintReport {
    46	  readonly total_nodes: number;
    47	  readonly total_edges: number;
    48	  readonly findings: readonly Finding[];
    49	  readonly by_category: ReadonlyMap<LintCategory, number>;
    50	}
    51	
    52	export interface LintOptions {
    53	  /** Restrict rules to nodes in a single room. Omit to lint everything. */
    54	  readonly room?: string;
    55	  /** Rooms the local peer has marked public via `share room`. Used by L7. */
    56	  readonly shared_rooms?: ReadonlySet<string>;
    57	  /** Pattern set for L8 drift check. Typically buildPatterns() result. */
    58	  readonly secret_patterns?: ReturnType<typeof buildPatterns>;
    59	}
    60	
    61	// ─────────────────────── rules ─────────────────────────────
    62	
    63	const ruleOrphans = (graph: Graph, scope: readonly GraphNode[]): readonly Finding[] =>
    64	  scope
    65	    .filter((n) => (graph.adjacency.get(n.id)?.size ?? 0) === 0)
    66	    .map((n): Finding => ({
    67	      category: 'orphan',
    68	      node_id: n.id,
    69	      room: typeof n.room === 'string' ? n.room : undefined,
    70	      detail: 'node has no incident edges',
    71	    }));
    72	
    73	const ruleDeadEdges = (graph: Graph): readonly Finding[] =>
    74	  graph.json.links
    75	    .filter((e) => !graph.nodeById.has(e.source) || !graph.nodeById.has(e.target))
    76	    .map((e): Finding => ({
    77	      category: 'dead-edge',
    78	      detail: `edge ${e.source} → ${e.target} references missing node`,
    79	    }));
    80	
    81	const ruleMissingRoom = (scope: readonly GraphNode[]): readonly Finding[] =>
    82	  scope
    83	    .filter((n) => typeof n.room !== 'string' || n.room.length === 0)
    84	    .map((n): Finding => ({
    85	      category: 'missing-room',
    86	      node_id: n.id,
    87	      detail: 'node has no room assignment',
    88	    }));
    89	
    90	const ruleMissingFetchedAt = (scope: readonly GraphNode[]): readonly Finding[] =>
    91	  scope
    92	    .filter((n) => typeof n.fetched_at !== 'string')
    93	    .map((n): Finding => ({
    94	      category: 'missing-fetched-at',
    95	      node_id: n.id,
    96	      room: typeof n.room === 'string' ? n.room : undefined,
    97	      detail: 'node has no fetched_at — age unknown',
    98	    }));
    99	
   100	const ruleEmptyLabel = (scope: readonly GraphNode[]): readonly Finding[] =>
   101	  scope
   102	    .filter((n) => typeof n.label !== 'string' || n.label.trim().length === 0)
   103	    .map((n): Finding => ({
   104	      category: 'empty-label',
   105	      node_id: n.id,
   106	      room: typeof n.room === 'string' ? n.room : undefined,
   107	      detail: 'node has empty or whitespace-only label',
   108	    }));
   109	
   110	const ruleDuplicateUri = (scope: readonly GraphNode[]): readonly Finding[] => {
   111	  const byUri = new Map<string, GraphNode[]>();
   112	  for (const n of scope) {
   113	    if (typeof n.source_uri !== 'string') continue;
   114	    const bucket = byUri.get(n.source_uri) ?? [];
   115	    bucket.push(n);
   116	    byUri.set(n.source_uri, bucket);
   117	  }
   118	  const findings: Finding[] = [];
   119	  for (const [uri, list] of byUri) {
   120	    if (list.length < 2) continue;
   121	    findings.push({
   122	      category: 'duplicate-uri',
   123	      detail: `${list.length} nodes share source_uri ${uri}: ${list.map((n) => n.id).join(', ').slice(0, 300)}`,
   124	    });
   125	  }
   126	  return findings;
   127	};
   128	
   129	const ruleUnsharedP2p = (
   130	  scope: readonly GraphNode[],
   131	  sharedRooms: ReadonlySet<string>,
   132	): readonly Finding[] =>
   133	  scope
   134	    .filter((n) => {
   135	      if (typeof n.source_file !== 'string') return false;
   136	      if (!n.source_file.startsWith('peer:') && !n.source_file.startsWith('p2p:')) return false;
   137	      const room = typeof n.room === 'string' ? n.room : '';
   138	      return !sharedRooms.has(room);
   139	    })
   140	    .map((n): Finding => ({
   141	      category: 'unshared-p2p',
   142	      node_id: n.id,
   143	      room: typeof n.room === 'string' ? n.room : undefined,
   144	      detail: 'node arrived from a peer but local room is not marked public — may indicate stale share-sync state',
   145	    }));
   146	
   147	const ruleStaleSecretMatch = (
   148	  scope: readonly GraphNode[],
   149	  patterns: ReturnType<typeof buildPatterns>,
   150	): readonly Finding[] =>
   151	  scope.flatMap((n): readonly Finding[] => {
   152	    const { redactions } = redactNode(n, patterns);
   153	    if (redactions.length === 0) return [];
   154	    const byName = redactions.map((r) => `${r.pattern_name}×${r.count}`).join(', ');
   155	    return [{
   156	      category: 'stale-secret-match',
   157	      node_id: n.id,
   158	      room: typeof n.room === 'string' ? n.room : undefined,
   159	      detail: `node text matches current secret patterns: ${byName}`,
   160	    }];
   161	  });
   162	
   163	// ─────────────────────── entry point ───────────────────────
   164	
   165	export const lintGraph = (graph: Graph, opts: LintOptions = {}): LintReport => {
   166	  const scope = opts.room
   167	    ? nodesInRoom(graph, opts.room)
   168	    : graph.json.nodes;
   169	
   170	  const findings: Finding[] = [];
   171	  findings.push(...ruleOrphans(graph, scope));
   172	  findings.push(...ruleDeadEdges(graph));
   173	  findings.push(...ruleMissingRoom(scope));
   174	  findings.push(...ruleMissingFetchedAt(scope));
   175	  findings.push(...ruleEmptyLabel(scope));
   176	  findings.push(...ruleDuplicateUri(scope));
   177	  if (opts.shared_rooms) findings.push(...ruleUnsharedP2p(scope, opts.shared_rooms));
   178	  if (opts.secret_patterns) findings.push(...ruleStaleSecretMatch(scope, opts.secret_patterns));
   179	
   180	  const byCategory = new Map<LintCategory, number>();
   181	  for (const f of findings) {
   182	    byCategory.set(f.category, (byCategory.get(f.category) ?? 0) + 1);
   183	  }
   184	  const totals = graphSize(graph);
   185	  return {
   186	    total_nodes: totals.nodes,
   187	    total_edges: totals.edges,
   188	    findings,
   189	    by_category: byCategory,
   190	  };

     1	/**
     2	 * Federated search — fan-out orchestrator for cross-peer semantic search.
     3	 *
     4	 * Phase 17 application layer. Coordinates:
     5	 *   1. Local vector query (searchGlobal or searchByRoom)
     6	 *   2. Parallel fan-out to all connected peers via openSearchStream
     7	 *   3. Result merging with deduplication (prefer local, collapse peer dupes into _also_from_peers)
     8	 *   4. Cross-room tunnel detection via findTunnels over the merged synthetic record set (FED-04)
     9	 *
    10	 * CRITICAL invariants:
    11	 *   1. Fan-out MUST use Promise.all with per-promise Promise.race timeout — NOT
    12	 *      ResultAsync.combine/ResultAsync.combineWithAllErrors which short-circuit on
    13	 *      first failure and block the entire fan-out (Research anti-pattern from 17-RESEARCH.md:
    14	 *      "Eager ResultAsync sequence on fan-out").
    15	 *
    16	 *   2. Per-peer timeout is 2000ms (CONTEXT.md locked). Degraded peers do not block
    17	 *      the query. Each peer outcome is tagged ok|timeout|error for diagnostics.
    18	 *
    19	 *   3. No Y.Doc mutations, no REMOTE_ORIGIN, no CRDT. This is a pure read-only path.
    20	 *
    21	 *   4. Tunnel detection (FED-04) runs over local vectors only for merged matches.
    22	 *      Remote-only rows are skipped because raw vectors are not transmitted across
    23	 *      the wire (SEC-03 boundary). This is a functional subset — documented, not a bug.
    24	 *
    25	 *   5. Dependency injection: openSearchStream is injectable (optional dep) for unit
    26	 *      testability. Tests can mock it to avoid real libp2p dials.
    27	 */
    28	import type { Libp2p } from '@libp2p/interface';
    29	import type { VectorIndex } from '../infrastructure/vector-index.js';
    30	import type { Vector, Tunnel, VectorRecord } from '../domain/vectors.js';
    31	import { findTunnels as findTunnelsPure } from '../domain/vectors.js';
    32	import type { Room } from '../domain/graph.js';
    33	import type { PeerMatch, SearchRequest } from '../infrastructure/search-sync.js';
    34	import { openSearchStream } from '../infrastructure/search-sync.js';
    35	import { askGossip } from '../infrastructure/search-gossip.js';
    36	
    37	// ─────────────────────── output types ─────────────────────────────────────────
    38	
    39	/**
    40	 * A merged search result — either local (source_peer=null) or
    41	 * from a specific remote peer (source_peer=peerId string).
    42	 *
    43	 * _also_from_peers lets the caller see which OTHER peers returned the
    44	 * same node_id. Deduplication prefers the local entry (or first-seen peer);
    45	 * same-node_id hits from additional peers are collapsed into _also_from_peers
    46	 * on the winning row. This is the Claude's-discretion decision from CONTEXT.md.
    47	 */
    48	export interface FederatedMatch {
    49	  readonly node_id: string;
    50	  readonly room: string;
    51	  readonly wing?: string;
    52	  readonly distance: number;
    53	  readonly _source_peer: string | null;
    54	  readonly _also_from_peers?: readonly string[];
    55	}
    56	
    57	export interface FederatedSearchResult {
    58	  readonly matches: readonly FederatedMatch[];
    59	  readonly tunnels: readonly Tunnel[];
    60	  readonly peers_queried: number;
    61	  readonly peers_responded: number;
    62	  readonly peers_timed_out: number;
    63	  readonly peers_errored: number;
    64	  /**
    65	   * Wire-level telemetry — populated unconditionally so callers can
    66	   * surface a peer-pull block into the agent session. Enrichment and
    67	   * satisfaction scoring happen at the call site (MCP / CLI / hook)
    68	   * where the GraphRepository is available.
    69	   */
    70	  readonly _telemetry: {
    71	    readonly took_total_ms: number;
    72	    readonly took_local_ms: number;
    73	    readonly took_fanout_ms: number;
    74	    readonly took_merge_ms: number;
    75	    readonly bytes_received_estimate: number;
    76	    readonly peers_alive: number;
    77	  };
    78	}
    79	
    80	export interface FederatedSearchDeps {
    81	  readonly node: Libp2p;
    82	  readonly vectorIndex: VectorIndex;
    83	  /**
    84	   * Injectable override for openSearchStream — allows unit tests to mock
    85	   * the outbound stream without a real libp2p node.
    86	   * Defaults to the real openSearchStream from search-sync.ts.
    87	   */
    88	  readonly openStream?: typeof openSearchStream;
    89	}
    90	
    91	export interface FederatedSearchParams {
    92	  readonly embedding: Vector;     // Float32Array, length === DEFAULT_DIM
    93	  readonly k: number;
    94	  readonly room?: string;
    95	  /**
    96	   * Raw query text for the local-half hybrid (vector + BM25) lookup. When
    97	   * omitted, local search falls back to vector-only. Peers still receive
    98	   * only the embedding (SEC-03 boundary — raw text does not cross the
    99	   * wire); this field exists for the LOCAL half, which has the text
   100	   * in-process anyway.
   101	   */
   102	  readonly text?: string;
   103	  /** Cross-room tunnel threshold. Default 0.6 matches MCP find_tunnels default. */
   104	  readonly tunnelThreshold?: number;
   105	  /**
   106	   * Skip the cross-room tunnel pass at the end of the merge.
   107	   * Tunnel detection runs findTunnelsPure on the merged result set,
   108	   * which on a 5–10 hit return adds ~150-250ms because it embeds
   109	   * pairs of records and compares. The agent contract block does
   110	   * not currently use tunnel output; --peers callers should set
   111	   * this to true to skip the cost.
   112	   */
   113	  readonly skipTunnels?: boolean;
   114	  /** Per-peer deadline. Default 2000ms matches CONTEXT.md locked decision. */
   115	  readonly perPeerTimeoutMs?: number;
   116	  /**
   117	   * Optional peer-ordering callback. When supplied, the connected
   118	   * peer list is passed through this function before fan-out — the
   119	   * reputation system uses it to bubble high-rep peers to the front
   120	   * (with an epsilon-greedy floor so unknown peers still get
   121	   * sampled). Pure: no I/O, no clock dependence at this layer; the
   122	   * caller closes any reputation/ranking state into the closure.
   123	   *
   124	   * Default behaviour (no callback) preserves the libp2p-native peer
   125	   * order — backwards compatible with every existing test.
   126	   */
   127	  readonly peerOrder?: (peerIds: readonly string[]) => readonly string[];
   128	  /**
   129	   * Optional cap on how many peers to fan out to. After `peerOrder`
   130	   * runs, the top `maxPeers` are queried; the rest are skipped.
   131	   *
   132	   * The mechanism that actually spreads load (combined with the rep
   133	   * system's load_factor in rank_score): top-rep peers get the
   134	   * fan-out budget; medium-rep peers stay idle this round; over time
   135	   * the load_factor decays a peer that's been hit recently and the
   136	   * rotation continues organically.
   137	   *
   138	   * Defaults to no cap (current behaviour — fan out to every
   139	   * connected peer). When set with a low rank-budget alongside, the
   140	   * combined effect is "ask top-3 peers fully; ask top-8 peers with
   141	   * a tighter timeout; skip the rest."
   142	   */
   143	  readonly maxPeers?: number;
   144	  /**
   145	   * Tier-2 timeout for peers ranked between TIER_1_COUNT and
   146	   * `maxPeers`. Lets the federated layer give your top-N peers the
   147	   * full 2 s budget while still sampling tier-2 peers under a
   148	   * shorter (e.g. 700 ms) deadline. When omitted, every peer gets
   149	   * the full `perPeerTimeoutMs`.
   150	   */
   151	  readonly lowRankTimeoutMs?: number;
   152	  /**
   153	   * How many peers count as the "top tier" that get the full
   154	   * `perPeerTimeoutMs`. Default 3 — every peer beyond this gets
   155	   * `lowRankTimeoutMs` if it's set. Ignored when `lowRankTimeoutMs`
   156	   * is undefined.
   157	   */
   158	  readonly topTierCount?: number;
   159	  /**
   160	   * P2P-scale phase 1 — use pubsub broadcast for fan-out instead of
   161	   * per-peer dialProtocol. Default true (gossip-first). When the
   162	   * gossip collector returns zero responses, the call falls through
   163	   * to the legacy per-peer dial path so a missing pubsub service
   164	   * never strands the request.
   165	   */
   166	  readonly useGossip?: boolean;
   167	  /**
   168	   * Collector window for gossip fan-out. Default 200 ms.
   169	   * Lower for tighter latency, higher for larger swarms (10k peers
   170	   * need ~300 ms floodsub propagation, ~80 ms gossipsub mesh).
   171	   */
   172	  readonly gossipWindowMs?: number;
   173	  /**
   174	   * Tail-aware merge cap (audit fold-in): never let a single peer
   175	   * contribute more than ⌈k/peerDiversityDivisor⌉ matches to the
   176	   * final top-k. Default 3 — i.e. top-3 peers collectively cannot
   177	   * monopolise more than k. Disabled by setting to Infinity.
   178	   */
   179	  readonly peerDiversityDivisor?: number;
   180	}
   181	
   182	// ─────────────────────── per-peer timeout helper ──────────────────────────────
   183	
   184	/**
   185	 * Outcome of a single peer's fan-out attempt.
   186	 * status: 'ok' = responded with matches (may be empty), 'timeout' = 2s exceeded,
   187	 *         'error' = dial or protocol error.
   188	 */
   189	interface PeerOutcome {
   190	  readonly peerId: string;
   191	  readonly status: 'ok' | 'timeout' | 'error';
   192	  readonly matches: ReadonlyArray<PeerMatch>;
   193	}
   194	
   195	/**
   196	 * Race a peer stream against a timeout.
   197	 *
   198	 * Pattern 2 (17-RESEARCH.md): Promise.race is the correct tool here —
   199	 * NOT ResultAsync.combine which short-circuits on first error, killing the
   200	 * fan-out for all remaining peers when one peer fails.
   201	 *
   202	 * Error handling: if `work` rejects, the outcome is tagged 'error' (not 'timeout').
   203	 * Both cases produce empty matches — the difference is visible in the diagnostic
   204	 * counters (peers_timed_out vs peers_errored) in FederatedSearchResult.
   205	 */
   206	const withTimeout = (
   207	  peerId: string,
   208	  work: Promise<ReadonlyArray<PeerMatch>>,
   209	  ms: number,
   210	): Promise<PeerOutcome> =>
   211	  Promise.race<PeerOutcome>([
   212	    work.then(
   213	      (matches) => ({ peerId, status: 'ok' as const, matches }),
   214	      () => ({ peerId, status: 'error' as const, matches: [] }),
   215	    ),
   216	    new Promise<PeerOutcome>((resolve) =>
   217	      setTimeout(() => resolve({ peerId, status: 'timeout' as const, matches: [] }), ms),
   218	    ),
   219	  ]);
   220	
   221	// ─────────────────────── tunnel detection helper ──────────────────────────────
   222	
   223	/**
   224	 * Cross-room tunnel pass over the merged match set.
   225	 *
   226	 * FED-04 implementation: "run existing findTunnels over the combined result set
   227	 * as a synthetic one-shot graph" (CONTEXT.md). Since raw vectors are not
   228	 * transmitted across the wire (SEC-03 boundary), we pull local vectors from the
   229	 * vector index for rows that exist locally and SKIP peer-only rows.
   230	 *
   231	 * This is a functional subset — documented, not a bug. Phase 18+ can add
   232	 * optional vector transmission for full cross-peer tunnel detection.
   233	 */
   234	const computeCrossRoomTunnels = async (
   235	  vectorIndex: VectorIndex,
   236	  merged: readonly FederatedMatch[],
   237	  threshold: number,
   238	): Promise<readonly Tunnel[]> => {
   239	  // Pull every local vector record once — acceptable for Phase 17 volumes.
   240	  // Phase 18+ could cache this or use a targeted per-node_id lookup.
   241	  const allRes = await vectorIndex.all();
   242	  if (allRes.isErr()) return [];
   243	  const localById = new Map<string, VectorRecord>();
   244	  for (const r of allRes.value) localById.set(r.node_id, r);
   245	
   246	  // Build the synthetic record set: for each merged match, use the local
   247	  // vector if available, skip peer-only rows (no vector on hand).
   248	  const synthetic: VectorRecord[] = [];
   249	  for (const m of merged) {
   250	    const local = localById.get(m.node_id);
   251	    if (local) synthetic.push(local);
   252	  }
   253	
   254	  // findTunnelsPure is O(n²) over the synthetic set (typically k ≤ 50 for Phase 17).
   255	  return findTunnelsPure(synthetic, threshold);
   256	};
   257	
   258	// ─────────────────────── main orchestrator ────────────────────────────────────
   259	
   260	/**

exec
/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba src/infrastructure/share-store.ts | sed -n '1,230p'" in /Users/saharbarak/personal/akashik
 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	/**
     2	 * Share store — persists the shared-rooms registry to ~/.akashik/shared-rooms.json.
     3	 * Mirrors peer-store.ts: cross-process lock + atomic tmp+rename writes + pure transforms.
     4	 *
     5	 * Phase 16 introduces this file alongside peers.json. Two separate registries
     6	 * (peers.json owns connections, shared-rooms.json owns the public rooms list)
     7	 * because their lifecycles and contention patterns are independent.
     8	 *
     9	 * Error types: SE.shareStoreReadError / SE.shareStoreWriteError for all I/O.
    10	 * Never throws to callers — all fallible ops return ResultAsync<_, ShareError>.
    11	 */
    12	import { ResultAsync, okAsync, errAsync } from 'neverthrow';
    13	import { readFile, writeFile, mkdir, rename, open, unlink } from 'node:fs/promises';
    14	import { existsSync } from 'node:fs';
    15	import { dirname } from 'node:path';
    16	import type { ShareError } from '../domain/errors.js';
    17	import { ShareError as SE } from '../domain/errors.js';
    18	import { SYSTEM_ROOMS, isSystemRoomName } from '../domain/system-rooms.js';
    19	
    20	/** Current shared-rooms.json schema version. Bump when making breaking changes. */
    21	const SHARED_ROOMS_VERSION = 2 as const;
    22	
    23	/**
    24	 * Cross-process lock for shared-rooms.json mutations.
    25	 *
    26	 * Uses exclusive-create (`wx` flag) on a sibling `.lock` file — POSIX
    27	 * atomic exclusive file creation with no external dependency. Holds the
    28	 * lock for the duration of a read-modify-write transaction so two
    29	 * concurrent akashik processes (e.g., daemon + CLI `share room foo`)
    30	 * cannot clobber each other's registry writes.
    31	 *
    32	 * Staleness guard: lock file contains the locker's PID and timestamp.
    33	 * Locks older than STALE_LOCK_MS are considered abandoned (e.g., parent
    34	 * process crashed) and are forcibly broken before a new locker acquires.
    35	 *
    36	 * Pattern is verbatim from peer-store.ts — the only behavioral difference
    37	 * is the error type returned on timeout (ShareStoreWriteError vs PeerStoreWriteError).
    38	 */
    39	const STALE_LOCK_MS = 30_000;
    40	const LOCK_RETRY_DELAY_MS = 50;
    41	const LOCK_MAX_WAIT_MS = 5_000;
    42	
    43	const sleep = (ms: number): Promise<void> => new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, ms));
    44	
    45	const isStaleLock = async (lockPath: string): Promise<boolean> => {
    46	  try {
    47	    const text = await readFile(lockPath, 'utf8');
    48	    const parsed = JSON.parse(text) as { pid?: number; timestamp?: number };
    49	    if (typeof parsed.timestamp !== 'number') return true;
    50	    return Date.now() - parsed.timestamp > STALE_LOCK_MS;
    51	  } catch {
    52	    // Unreadable lock file — treat as stale so we don't deadlock forever
    53	    return true;
    54	  }
    55	};
    56	
    57	const acquireLock = async (lockPath: string): Promise<void> => {
    58	  const deadline = Date.now() + LOCK_MAX_WAIT_MS;
    59	  while (true) {
    60	    try {
    61	      const handle = await open(lockPath, 'wx');
    62	      await handle.writeFile(
    63	        JSON.stringify({ pid: process.pid, timestamp: Date.now() }),
    64	      );
    65	      await handle.close();
    66	      return;
    67	    } catch (e) {
    68	      const err = e as NodeJS.ErrnoException;
    69	      if (err.code !== 'EEXIST') throw err;
    70	      // Lock exists — check if stale
    71	      if (await isStaleLock(lockPath)) {
    72	        try {
    73	          await unlink(lockPath);
    74	        } catch {
    75	          // Another process may have cleaned it up — retry
    76	        }
    77	        continue;
    78	      }
    79	      if (Date.now() >= deadline) {
    80	        throw new Error(`timed out waiting for shared-rooms.json lock after ${LOCK_MAX_WAIT_MS}ms`);
    81	      }
    82	      await sleep(LOCK_RETRY_DELAY_MS);
    83	    }
    84	  }
    85	};
    86	
    87	const releaseLock = async (lockPath: string): Promise<void> => {
    88	  try {
    89	    await unlink(lockPath);
    90	  } catch {
    91	    // Lock already gone — benign
    92	  }
    93	};
    94	
    95	// ─────────────────────── types ────────────────────────────
    96	
    97	export interface SharedRoomRecord {
    98	  /** Room name as used by the local graph (room field on GraphNode). */
    99	  readonly name: string;
   100	  /** ISO-8601 timestamp when this room was first shared. */
   101	  readonly sharedAt: string;
   102	  /**
   103	   * Phase 20 — non-shareable rooms hard-refuse `share room <name>`.
   104	   * Legacy v1 records without this field are treated as shareable: true on read
   105	   * (backwards compatibility). The `sessions` room is explicitly created with
   106	   * shareable: false so session data never crosses libp2p.
   107	   */
   108	  readonly shareable: boolean;
   109	}
   110	
   111	export interface SharedRoomsFile {
   112	  /** Schema version — present so future format changes can migrate safely. */
   113	  readonly version: number;
   114	  readonly rooms: readonly SharedRoomRecord[];
   115	}
   116	
   117	// ─────────────────────── constants ────────────────────────
   118	
   119	const EMPTY_FILE: SharedRoomsFile = Object.freeze({ version: SHARED_ROOMS_VERSION, rooms: [] });
   120	
   121	// ─────────────────────── I/O operations ───────────────────
   122	
   123	/**
   124	 * Load shared rooms from disk. Returns an empty registry if the file does
   125	 * not exist. Returns `err(shareStoreReadError)` if the file exists but is
   126	 * unparseable or shape-invalid — silently swallowing corruption would let
   127	 * the next `saveSharedRooms` overwrite the user's registry with empty.
   128	 *
   129	 * Accepts both current (`version: 1`) and legacy (no version) shapes for
   130	 * forward compatibility, but always writes the current version on save.
   131	 */
   132	export const loadSharedRooms = (
   133	  path: string,
   134	): ResultAsync<SharedRoomsFile, ShareError> => {
   135	  if (!existsSync(path)) return okAsync(EMPTY_FILE);
   136	  return ResultAsync.fromPromise(
   137	    readFile(path, 'utf8'),
   138	    (e) => SE.shareStoreReadError(path, (e as Error).message),
   139	  ).andThen((text) => {
   140	    let parsed: unknown;
   141	    try {
   142	      parsed = JSON.parse(text);
   143	    } catch (e) {
   144	      return errAsync<SharedRoomsFile, ShareError>(
   145	        SE.shareStoreReadError(path, `parse failed: ${(e as Error).message}`),
   146	      );
   147	    }
   148	    if (!parsed || typeof parsed !== 'object') {
   149	      return errAsync<SharedRoomsFile, ShareError>(
   150	        SE.shareStoreReadError(path, 'root must be an object'),
   151	      );
   152	    }
   153	    const obj = parsed as Record<string, unknown>;
   154	    if (!Array.isArray(obj.rooms)) {
   155	      return errAsync<SharedRoomsFile, ShareError>(
   156	        SE.shareStoreReadError(path, "missing or invalid 'rooms' array"),
   157	      );
   158	    }
   159	    const version = typeof obj.version === 'number' ? obj.version : SHARED_ROOMS_VERSION;
   160	    if (version > SHARED_ROOMS_VERSION) {
   161	      return errAsync<SharedRoomsFile, ShareError>(
   162	        SE.shareStoreReadError(
   163	          path,
   164	          `unsupported shared-rooms.json version ${version} (supported: ${SHARED_ROOMS_VERSION})`,
   165	        ),
   166	      );
   167	    }
   168	    const normalised: SharedRoomRecord[] = (
   169	      obj.rooms as readonly Partial<SharedRoomRecord>[]
   170	    ).map((r) => ({
   171	      name: String(r.name ?? ''),
   172	      sharedAt: String(r.sharedAt ?? ''),
   173	      shareable: typeof r.shareable === 'boolean' ? r.shareable : true,
   174	    }));
   175	    return okAsync<SharedRoomsFile, ShareError>({
   176	      version: SHARED_ROOMS_VERSION,
   177	      rooms: normalised,
   178	    });
   179	  });
   180	};
   181	
   182	/**
   183	 * Persist shared rooms to disk atomically: write to a .tmp file then rename
   184	 * into the final path. Rename is atomic on POSIX — readers see either
   185	 * the old complete file or the new complete file, never a partial write.
   186	 */
   187	export const saveSharedRooms = (
   188	  path: string,
   189	  file: SharedRoomsFile,
   190	): ResultAsync<void, ShareError> => {
   191	  const dir = dirname(path);
   192	  const tmp = `${path}.tmp`;
   193	  return ResultAsync.fromPromise(
   194	    mkdir(dir, { recursive: true }),
   195	    (e) => SE.shareStoreWriteError(path, (e as Error).message),
   196	  ).andThen(() =>
   197	    ResultAsync.fromPromise(
   198	      writeFile(tmp, JSON.stringify(file, null, 2), 'utf8'),
   199	      (e) => SE.shareStoreWriteError(path, (e as Error).message),
   200	    ),
   201	  ).andThen(() =>
   202	    ResultAsync.fromPromise(
   203	      rename(tmp, path),
   204	      (e) => SE.shareStoreWriteError(path, (e as Error).message),
   205	    ),
   206	  );
   207	};
   208	
   209	// ─────────────────────── pure transforms ──────────────────
   210	
   211	/**
   212	 * Add a shared room record. If the room already exists (by name), updates
   213	 * its record (idempotent — `share room foo` twice keeps a single entry).
   214	 * Returns a new SharedRoomsFile — caller is responsible for persisting
   215	 * via saveSharedRooms.
   216	 */
   217	export const addSharedRoom = (
   218	  file: SharedRoomsFile,
   219	  record: SharedRoomRecord,
   220	): SharedRoomsFile => {
   221	  const exists = file.rooms.some((r) => r.name === record.name);
   222	  if (exists) {
   223	    return {
   224	      version: file.version,
   225	      rooms: file.rooms.map((r) => (r.name === record.name ? record : r)),
   226	    };
   227	  }
   228	  return { version: file.version, rooms: [...file.rooms, record] };
   229	};
   230

 succeeded in 0ms:
   230	
   231	/**
   232	 * Remove a shared room record by name. Returns unchanged file if the name
   233	 * is not found — idempotent, never an error. Caller is responsible for
   234	 * persisting via saveSharedRooms.
   235	 */
   236	export const removeSharedRoom = (
   237	  file: SharedRoomsFile,
   238	  name: string,
   239	): SharedRoomsFile => {
   240	  // System-managed rooms (toolshed / research) are P2P-always-shared
   241	  // by design — refusing to remove them keeps the out-of-the-box
   242	  // surface every peer can count on.
   243	  if (isSystemRoomName(name)) return file;
   244	  return {
   245	    version: file.version,
   246	    rooms: file.rooms.filter((r) => r.name !== name),
   247	  };
   248	};
   249	
   250	/**
   251	 * Boot-time invariant: the two system rooms (toolshed, research) are
   252	 * always present in shared-rooms.json with shareable: true. Callers
   253	 * run this on CLI startup / daemon boot so a fresh install or a file
   254	 * rolled forward from an older version picks them up automatically.
   255	 *
   256	 * Idempotent — no-op when both rooms are already present + shareable.
   257	 */
   258	export const ensureSystemRoomsShared = (
   259	  path: string,
   260	  now: Date = new Date(),
   261	): ResultAsync<SharedRoomsFile, ShareError> =>
   262	  mutateSharedRooms(path, (current) => {
   263	    const iso = now.toISOString();
   264	    let out: SharedRoomsFile = current;
   265	    for (const spec of SYSTEM_ROOMS) {
   266	      const existing = out.rooms.find((r) => r.name === spec.name);
   267	      const desired: SharedRoomRecord = {
   268	        name: spec.name,
   269	        sharedAt: existing?.sharedAt ?? iso,
   270	        shareable: true,
   271	      };
   272	      out = addSharedRoom(out, desired);
   273	    }
   274	    return out;
   275	  });
   276	
   277	// ─────────────────────── transactional mutate ─────────────
   278	
   279	/**
   280	 * Transactional read-modify-write for shared-rooms.json.
   281	 *
   282	 * Acquires a cross-process lock (sibling `.lock` file via exclusive-create),
   283	 * loads the current state, applies the pure transform, saves atomically,
   284	 * and releases the lock. Prevents two akashik processes (e.g., daemon +
   285	 * CLI `share room foo`) from racing and clobbering each other's writes.
   286	 *
   287	 * The `transform` function receives the current SharedRoomsFile and must
   288	 * return the new SharedRoomsFile — it should be pure (no I/O). All side
   289	 * effects happen outside the transform, wrapped by this function.
   290	 *
   291	 * On lock acquisition failure (timeout or abandoned stale lock), returns
   292	 * SE.shareStoreWriteError with a descriptive message. Callers should treat
   293	 * lock errors as retryable at a higher level.
   294	 */
   295	export const mutateSharedRooms = (
   296	  path: string,
   297	  transform: (current: SharedRoomsFile) => SharedRoomsFile,
   298	): ResultAsync<SharedRoomsFile, ShareError> => {
   299	  const lockPath = `${path}.lock`;
   300	  return ResultAsync.fromPromise(
   301	    acquireLock(lockPath),
   302	    (e) => SE.shareStoreWriteError(path, `lock acquire failed: ${(e as Error).message}`),
   303	  )
   304	    .andThen(() => loadSharedRooms(path))
   305	    .andThen((current) => {
   306	      const next = transform(current);
   307	      return saveSharedRooms(path, next).map(() => next);
   308	    })
   309	    .andThen((result) =>
   310	      ResultAsync.fromPromise(
   311	        releaseLock(lockPath),
   312	        (e) => SE.shareStoreWriteError(path, `lock release failed: ${(e as Error).message}`),
   313	      ).map(() => result),
   314	    )
   315	    .orElse((err) =>
   316	      // Best-effort lock release on failure path — don't mask the original error
   317	      ResultAsync.fromPromise(
   318	        releaseLock(lockPath),
   319	        () => err,
   320	      ).andThen(() => errAsync(err)),
   321	    );
   322	};

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	/**
     2	 * System-managed rooms — the out-of-the-box rooms every akashik
     3	 * node carries and shares over P2P without user opt-in.
     4	 *
     5	 * Two rooms:
     6	 *   toolshed  — the agent's capabilities: codebase nodes, skills, MCP
     7	 *               tool descriptors, dependency graph, git history. This
     8	 *               is "what can this peer do / what do they know how to
     9	 *               do" — it's what lets another peer treat this one as a
    10	 *               useful counterparty.
    11	 *   research  — the agent's external reading: arxiv, hn, rss, web
    12	 *               fetches, web searches, telegram captures. "What has
    13	 *               this peer recently read about" — the epistemic surface.
    14	 *
    15	 * Design choices:
    16	 *
    17	 * 1. Virtual, not physical. A node's membership in toolshed/research is
    18	 *    derived from its `source_uri` scheme, not set by a `room` field.
    19	 *    A git commit tagged `room: akashik-dev` is STILL in toolshed.
    20	 *    This avoids a schema migration and preserves the user's existing
    21	 *    room taxonomy. Users still see the physical room in their graph;
    22	 *    the system room is just a query-time lens over the same nodes.
    23	 *
    24	 * 2. Always shared. Both rooms are pinned into shared-rooms.json at
    25	 *    boot, marked `shareable: true, systemManaged: true`. Unshare
    26	 *    commands refuse to remove them. This is the always-available
    27	 *    surface the P2P network relies on — every peer can touch the
    28	 *    other's toolshed + research without negotiating individual rooms.
    29	 *
    30	 * 3. Age-gated. Each system room declares `staleAfterDays`. The touch
    31	 *    responder sorts by `fetched_at DESC` before returning, and every
    32	 *    node on the wire carries `fetched_at` (required, enforced by the
    33	 *    remote-node validator). The initiator's LLM reads `age_days` off
    34	 *    each hit and decides "trust the cache" vs "re-fetch fresh" per
    35	 *    the rule documented in CLAUDE.md.
    36	 *
    37	 * No other rooms are system-managed. Every user room is negotiable —
    38	 * opt-in for P2P sharing via the interactive `akashik share` TUI.
    39	 */
    40	
    41	import type { GraphNode } from './graph.js';
    42	
    43	// ─────────────────────── types ────────────────────────────
    44	
    45	export type SystemRoomName = 'toolshed' | 'research' | 'oracle';
    46	
    47	export interface SystemRoomSpec {
    48	  readonly name: SystemRoomName;
    49	  readonly description: string;
    50	  /** Node is stale when `now - fetched_at > staleAfterDays`. LLMs read this
    51	   *  to decide whether a graph hit is fresh enough vs a re-fetch. */
    52	  readonly staleAfterDays: number;
    53	  /** URI-scheme prefixes whose nodes belong to this virtual room. Matched
    54	   *  against node.source_uri via startsWith — single source of truth for
    55	   *  membership. */
    56	  readonly uriPrefixes: readonly string[];
    57	}
    58	
    59	// ─────────────────────── specs ────────────────────────────
    60	
    61	export const TOOLSHED: SystemRoomSpec = Object.freeze({
    62	  name: 'toolshed',
    63	  description: 'codebase, skills, MCP tools, deps, git history — the agent\'s capabilities',
    64	  staleAfterDays: 30,
    65	  uriPrefixes: ['git:', 'npm:', 'file-uri:', 'file://', 'skill:', 'mcp-tool:', 'repo:'],
    66	});
    67	
    68	export const RESEARCH: SystemRoomSpec = Object.freeze({
    69	  name: 'research',
    70	  description: 'arxiv, hn, rss, web searches, web fetches, telegram — external reading',
    71	  staleAfterDays: 7,
    72	  uriPrefixes: ['arxiv:', 'hn:', 'rss:', 'websearch:', 'http://', 'https://', 'telegram:'],
    73	});
    74	
    75	/** Bulletin-board room for peer-to-peer Q&A. Questions and answers live
    76	 *  here as nodes (schemes oracle-question: / oracle-answer:) and
    77	 *  propagate via the existing touch + CRDT surface. Async by design —
    78	 *  no new protocol code. Stale-after: 14 days (a question that's been
    79	 *  open two weeks is probably not getting answered organically). */
    80	export const ORACLE: SystemRoomSpec = Object.freeze({
    81	  name: 'oracle',
    82	  description: 'peer-to-peer Q&A bulletin board — questions and answers propagate via touch/CRDT',
    83	  staleAfterDays: 14,
    84	  uriPrefixes: ['oracle-question:', 'oracle-answer:'],
    85	});
    86	
    87	export const SYSTEM_ROOMS: readonly SystemRoomSpec[] = Object.freeze([TOOLSHED, RESEARCH, ORACLE]);
    88	
    89	export const SYSTEM_ROOM_NAMES: ReadonlySet<string> =
    90	  new Set(SYSTEM_ROOMS.map((r) => r.name));
    91	
    92	export const isSystemRoomName = (name: string): name is SystemRoomName =>
    93	  SYSTEM_ROOM_NAMES.has(name);
    94	
    95	export const findSystemRoom = (name: string): SystemRoomSpec | undefined =>
    96	  SYSTEM_ROOMS.find((r) => r.name === name);
    97	
    98	// ─────────────────────── membership ────────────────────────
    99	
   100	/**
   101	 * Does this node belong to the given system room? Derived from
   102	 * source_uri scheme — NOT from the node's own `room` field.
   103	 *
   104	 * Falsy source_uri → false (can't classify a node without a source).
   105	 */
   106	export const belongsToSystemRoom = (node: GraphNode, spec: SystemRoomSpec): boolean => {
   107	  const uri = typeof node.source_uri === 'string' ? node.source_uri : undefined;
   108	  if (!uri) return false;
   109	  return spec.uriPrefixes.some((p) => uri.startsWith(p));
   110	};
   111	
   112	/**
   113	 * All nodes in a system room, newest-first by fetched_at. Nodes without
   114	 * `fetched_at` go last (they're un-aged, which is worse than known-stale).
   115	 *
   116	 * `isolatedRooms` is the opt-out list — physical room names the user
   117	 * has marked `shareable: false` in shared-rooms.json. Nodes whose
   118	 * physical `room` is in that set are excluded from system-room
   119	 * membership even if their source_uri scheme matches. This is the
   120	 * escape hatch that keeps `room: private` content off the wire.
   121	 */
   122	export const nodesInSystemRoom = (
   123	  nodes: readonly GraphNode[],
   124	  spec: SystemRoomSpec,
   125	  isolatedRooms: ReadonlySet<string> = new Set(),
   126	): readonly GraphNode[] => {
   127	  const keep = nodes.filter((n) => {
   128	    if (!belongsToSystemRoom(n, spec)) return false;
   129	    if (typeof n.room === 'string' && isolatedRooms.has(n.room)) return false;
   130	    return true;
   131	  });
   132	  return [...keep].sort((a, b) => {
   133	    const ta = typeof a.fetched_at === 'string' ? Date.parse(a.fetched_at) : 0;
   134	    const tb = typeof b.fetched_at === 'string' ? Date.parse(b.fetched_at) : 0;
   135	    return tb - ta;
   136	  });
   137	};
   138	
   139	// ─────────────────────── aging ────────────────────────────
   140	
   141	/**
   142	 * Days since fetched_at, or null if the node has no timestamp.
   143	 * Fractional for sub-day precision; UTC-based; tolerates bad strings.
   144	 */
   145	export const ageDays = (node: GraphNode, now: Date = new Date()): number | null => {
   146	  const ts = typeof node.fetched_at === 'string' ? Date.parse(node.fetched_at) : NaN;
   147	  if (!Number.isFinite(ts)) return null;
   148	  const deltaMs = now.getTime() - ts;
   149	  return deltaMs / 86_400_000;
   150	};
   151	
   152	/** True when a node in `spec` is past its staleness window. */
   153	export const isStale = (node: GraphNode, spec: SystemRoomSpec, now: Date = new Date()): boolean => {
   154	  const age = ageDays(node, now);
   155	  if (age === null) return true; // un-aged = worst case
   156	  return age > spec.staleAfterDays;
   157	};
