/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba src/cli/commands/init.ts | sed -n '1,260p'" in /Users/saharbarak/personal/akashik
 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	/**
     2	 * `akashik init` — interactive room seeding wizard.
     3	 *
     4	 * Walks the user through:
     5	 *   1. What are you researching? → room name + description
     6	 *   2. Key topics / keywords → used to suggest sources
     7	 *   3. Do you have RSS feeds? → adds generic_rss sources
     8	 *   4. Search ArXiv? → adds arxiv source with derived query
     9	 *   5. Search HN? → adds hn_algolia source with derived query
    10	 *   6. Any specific URLs to seed? → adds generic_url sources
    11	 *   7. Summary + confirmation
    12	 *
    13	 * The wizard talks to the room and source registries. It is
    14	 * testable via the `Prompter` interface — tests inject answers
    15	 * instead of using readline.
    16	 *
    17	 * Non-interactive mode: `akashik init --name X --desc Y --keywords a,b`
    18	 * skips all prompts and creates the room + default sources from flags.
    19	 */
    20	
    21	import { createInterface, type Interface } from 'node:readline';
    22	import { join } from 'node:path';
    23	import { formatError } from '../../domain/errors.js';
    24	import type { RoomMeta } from '../../domain/rooms.js';
    25	import { slugifyRoomName } from '../../domain/rooms.js';
    26	import type { SourceDescriptor, SourceKind } from '../../domain/sources.js';
    27	import { fileRoomsConfig } from '../../infrastructure/rooms-config.js';
    28	import { fileSourcesConfig } from '../../infrastructure/sources-config.js';
    29	import { runtimePaths } from '../runtime.js';
    30	
    31	// ─────────────── prompter port ──────────
    32	
    33	export interface Prompter {
    34	  ask(question: string, defaultValue?: string): Promise<string>;
    35	  confirm(question: string, defaultYes?: boolean): Promise<boolean>;
    36	  close(): void;
    37	}
    38	
    39	export const readlinePrompter = (): Prompter => {
    40	  const rl: Interface = createInterface({ input: process.stdin, output: process.stdout });
    41	  const ask = (question: string, defaultValue?: string): Promise<string> =>
    42	    new Promise((resolve) => {
    43	      const suffix = defaultValue ? ` [${defaultValue}]` : '';
    44	      rl.question(`  ${question}${suffix}: `, (answer) => {
    45	        resolve(answer.trim() || defaultValue || '');
    46	      });
    47	    });
    48	  const confirm = (question: string, defaultYes = true): Promise<boolean> =>
    49	    new Promise((resolve) => {
    50	      const hint = defaultYes ? '[Y/n]' : '[y/N]';
    51	      rl.question(`  ${question} ${hint}: `, (answer) => {
    52	        const a = answer.trim().toLowerCase();
    53	        if (a === '') resolve(defaultYes);
    54	        else resolve(a === 'y' || a === 'yes');
    55	      });
    56	    });
    57	  const close = (): void => rl.close();
    58	  return { ask, confirm, close };
    59	};
    60	
    61	/**
    62	 * Builds a non-interactive prompter from pre-supplied answers.
    63	 * Used by tests and the `--name/--desc/--keywords` flag path.
    64	 */
    65	export const staticPrompter = (answers: readonly string[]): Prompter => {
    66	  let idx = 0;
    67	  const ask = async (): Promise<string> => answers[idx++] ?? '';
    68	  const confirm = async (): Promise<boolean> => {
    69	    const a = answers[idx++] ?? 'y';
    70	    return a.toLowerCase() === 'y' || a.toLowerCase() === 'yes';
    71	  };
    72	  const close = (): void => {};
    73	  return { ask, confirm, close };
    74	};
    75	
    76	// ─────────────── flag parsing ───────────
    77	
    78	interface InitFlags {
    79	  readonly name?: string;
    80	  readonly desc?: string;
    81	  readonly keywords?: string;
    82	  readonly rss?: string;
    83	  readonly arxiv?: boolean;
    84	  readonly hn?: boolean;
    85	}
    86	
    87	const parseFlags = (args: readonly string[]): InitFlags => {
    88	  const flags: Record<string, string | boolean> = {};
    89	  for (let i = 0; i < args.length; i++) {
    90	    const a = args[i];
    91	    const next = (): string => args[++i] ?? '';
    92	    if (a === '--name') flags.name = next();
    93	    else if (a.startsWith('--name=')) flags.name = a.slice('--name='.length);
    94	    else if (a === '--desc') flags.desc = next();
    95	    else if (a.startsWith('--desc=')) flags.desc = a.slice('--desc='.length);
    96	    else if (a === '--keywords') flags.keywords = next();
    97	    else if (a.startsWith('--keywords=')) flags.keywords = a.slice('--keywords='.length);
    98	    else if (a === '--rss') flags.rss = next();
    99	    else if (a.startsWith('--rss=')) flags.rss = a.slice('--rss='.length);
   100	    else if (a === '--arxiv') flags.arxiv = true;
   101	    else if (a === '--no-arxiv') flags.arxiv = false;
   102	    else if (a === '--hn') flags.hn = true;
   103	    else if (a === '--no-hn') flags.hn = false;
   104	  }
   105	  return flags as InitFlags;
   106	};
   107	
   108	// ─────────────── wizard ─────────────────
   109	
   110	const runWizard = async (
   111	  prompter: Prompter,
   112	  flags: InitFlags,
   113	): Promise<{
   114	  room: RoomMeta;
   115	  sources: readonly SourceDescriptor[];
   116	} | null> => {
   117	  console.log('\nakashik init — set up a new research room\n');
   118	
   119	  // 1. Name
   120	  const name = flags.name ?? (await prompter.ask('What are you researching?', 'homelab'));
   121	  if (!name) {
   122	    console.log('no room name provided. aborting.');
   123	    return null;
   124	  }
   125	  const id = slugifyRoomName(name);
   126	
   127	  // 2. Description
   128	  const desc = flags.desc ?? (await prompter.ask('Short description', `Research room for ${name}`));
   129	
   130	  // 3. Keywords
   131	  const keywordsStr =
   132	    flags.keywords ?? (await prompter.ask('Keywords (comma-separated)', name.toLowerCase()));
   133	  const keywords = keywordsStr
   134	    .split(',')
   135	    .map((k) => k.trim())
   136	    .filter(Boolean);
   137	
   138	  const room: RoomMeta = {
   139	    id,
   140	    name,
   141	    description: desc,
   142	    keywords,
   143	    created_at: new Date().toISOString(),
   144	  };
   145	
   146	  console.log(`\n  room: ${id}`);
   147	  console.log(`  desc: ${desc}`);
   148	  console.log(`  keywords: ${keywords.join(', ')}\n`);
   149	
   150	  // 4. Sources
   151	  const sources: SourceDescriptor[] = [];
   152	
   153	  // RSS
   154	  const rssUrl = flags.rss ?? (await prompter.ask('RSS/Atom feed URL? (blank to skip)'));
   155	  if (rssUrl) {
   156	    sources.push({
   157	      id: `${id}-rss`,
   158	      kind: 'generic_rss' as SourceKind,
   159	      room: id,
   160	      enabled: true,
   161	      config: { feed_url: rssUrl, max_items: 20 },
   162	    });
   163	    console.log(`  + generic_rss: ${rssUrl}`);
   164	  }
   165	
   166	  // ArXiv
   167	  const useArxiv = flags.arxiv ?? (await prompter.confirm(`Search ArXiv for "${keywords.join(' OR ')}"?`));
   168	  if (useArxiv) {
   169	    const query = keywords.map((k) => `abs:${k}`).join(' OR ');
   170	    sources.push({
   171	      id: `${id}-arxiv`,
   172	      kind: 'arxiv' as SourceKind,
   173	      room: id,
   174	      enabled: true,
   175	      config: { query, max_items: 10 },
   176	    });
   177	    console.log(`  + arxiv: ${query}`);
   178	  }
   179	
   180	  // HN
   181	  const useHn = flags.hn ?? (await prompter.confirm(`Search Hacker News for "${keywords.join(' ')}"?`));
   182	  if (useHn) {
   183	    sources.push({
   184	      id: `${id}-hn`,
   185	      kind: 'hn_algolia' as SourceKind,
   186	      room: id,
   187	      enabled: true,
   188	      config: { query: keywords.join(' '), max_items: 15, tags: 'story' },
   189	    });
   190	    console.log(`  + hn_algolia: ${keywords.join(' ')}`);
   191	  }
   192	
   193	  console.log(`\n  total sources: ${sources.length}`);
   194	  return { room, sources };
   195	};
   196	
   197	// ─────────────── entry ──────────────────
   198	
   199	export const init = async (args: readonly string[]): Promise<number> => {
   200	  const flags = parseFlags(args);
   201	  const isNonInteractive = Boolean(flags.name);
   202	  const prompter = isNonInteractive
   203	    ? staticPrompter([]) // flags supply all values
   204	    : readlinePrompter();
   205	
   206	  try {
   207	    const result = await runWizard(prompter, flags);
   208	    if (!result) return 1;
   209	
   210	    const paths = runtimePaths();
   211	    const rooms = fileRoomsConfig(join(paths.home, 'rooms.json'));
   212	    const sources = fileSourcesConfig(paths.sources);
   213	
   214	    // Create room
   215	    const roomResult = await rooms.create(result.room);
   216	    if (roomResult.isErr()) {
   217	      console.error(`init: ${formatError(roomResult.error)}`);
   218	      return 1;
   219	    }
   220	
   221	    // Register sources
   222	    for (const s of result.sources) {
   223	      const sr = await sources.add(s);
   224	      if (sr.isErr()) {
   225	        console.error(`init: failed to add source ${s.id}: ${formatError(sr.error)}`);
   226	      }
   227	    }
   228	
   229	    console.log(`\nroom '${result.room.id}' created with ${result.sources.length} source(s).`);
   230	    console.log(`run 'akashik trigger --room ${result.room.id}' to fetch initial content.`);
   231	    return 0;
   232	  } finally {
   233	    prompter.close();
   234	  }
   235	};

     1	/**
     2	 * `akashik index [--room R] [--root DIR]`
     3	 *
     4	 * Index the current project into the knowledge graph: source files,
     5	 * package.json dependencies, git submodules, and recent git history.
     6	 *
     7	 * This command creates ephemeral source descriptors for the four
     8	 * project-indexing adapters (codebase, package_deps, git_submodules,
     9	 * git_log), runs them through the ingest pipeline, and reports
    10	 * what was indexed. The descriptors are NOT persisted to sources.json
    11	 * — they're derived from the working directory each time.
    12	 *
    13	 * By default it uses the default room from the room registry. If no
    14	 * room exists, it creates one named after the project directory.
    15	 */
    16	
    17	import { basename } from 'node:path';
    18	import { formatError } from '../../domain/errors.js';
    19	import type { SourceDescriptor } from '../../domain/sources.js';
    20	import { slugifyRoomName, defaultRoom } from '../../domain/rooms.js';
    21	import { ingestSource } from '../../application/ingest.js';
    22	import { defaultRuntime } from '../runtime.js';
    23	
    24	interface ParsedArgs {
    25	  readonly room?: string;
    26	  readonly root: string;
    27	  readonly includeDev: boolean;
    28	  readonly maxCommits: number;
    29	}
    30	
    31	const parseArgs = (args: readonly string[]): ParsedArgs => {
    32	  let room: string | undefined;
    33	  let root = process.cwd();
    34	  let includeDev = true;
    35	  let maxCommits = 50;
    36	  for (let i = 0; i < args.length; i++) {
    37	    const a = args[i];
    38	    const next = (): string => args[++i] ?? '';
    39	    if (a === '--room') room = next();
    40	    else if (a.startsWith('--room=')) room = a.slice('--room='.length);
    41	    else if (a === '--root') root = next();
    42	    else if (a.startsWith('--root=')) root = a.slice('--root='.length);
    43	    else if (a === '--include-dev') includeDev = true;
    44	    else if (a === '--no-dev') includeDev = false;
    45	    else if (a === '--max-commits') maxCommits = parseInt(next(), 10) || 50;
    46	  }
    47	  return { room, root, includeDev, maxCommits };
    48	};
    49	
    50	export const indexProject = async (args: readonly string[]): Promise<number> => {
    51	  const parsed = parseArgs(args);
    52	
    53	  const rt = await defaultRuntime();
    54	  if (rt.isErr()) {
    55	    console.error(`index: ${formatError(rt.error)}`);
    56	    return 1;
    57	  }
    58	  const runtime = rt.value;
    59	
    60	  try {
    61	    // Resolve room — use flag, default room, or derive from directory name
    62	    let room = parsed.room;
    63	    if (!room) {
    64	      const reg = await runtime.rooms.load();
    65	      if (reg.isOk()) {
    66	        room = defaultRoom(reg.value);
    67	      }
    68	    }
    69	    if (!room) {
    70	      room = slugifyRoomName(basename(parsed.root));
    71	      // Auto-create the room
    72	      await runtime.rooms.create({
    73	        id: room,
    74	        name: basename(parsed.root),
    75	        description: `Project index for ${basename(parsed.root)}`,
    76	        keywords: [],
    77	        created_at: new Date().toISOString(),
    78	      });
    79	      console.log(`auto-created room '${room}'`);
    80	    }
    81	
    82	    // Build project-indexing descriptors (ephemeral, not saved)
    83	    const descriptors: SourceDescriptor[] = [
    84	      {
    85	        id: `${room}-codebase`,
    86	        kind: 'codebase',
    87	        room,
    88	        enabled: true,
    89	        config: { root: parsed.root },
    90	      },
    91	      {
    92	        id: `${room}-deps`,
    93	        kind: 'package_deps',
    94	        room,
    95	        enabled: true,
    96	        config: { root: parsed.root, include_dev: parsed.includeDev },
    97	      },
    98	      {
    99	        id: `${room}-submodules`,
   100	        kind: 'git_submodules',
   101	        room,
   102	        enabled: true,
   103	        config: { root: parsed.root },
   104	      },
   105	      {
   106	        id: `${room}-git`,
   107	        kind: 'git_log',
   108	        room,
   109	        enabled: true,
   110	        config: { root: parsed.root, max_commits: parsed.maxCommits },
   111	      },
   112	    ];
   113	
   114	    console.log(`indexing project at ${parsed.root} → room=${room}\n`);
   115	
   116	    const ingest = ingestSource(runtime.ingestDeps);
   117	    let totalNew = 0;
   118	    let totalSkipped = 0;
   119	    let hadError = false;
   120	
   121	    for (const desc of descriptors) {
   122	      const { sources: srcs, errors } = runtime.registry.buildAll([desc]);
   123	      if (errors.length > 0 || srcs.length === 0) {
   124	        console.error(`  [fail] ${desc.kind.padEnd(16)} — ${errors.map(formatError).join('; ')}`);
   125	        hadError = true;
   126	        continue;
   127	      }
   128	      const result = await ingest(srcs[0]);
   129	      if (result.isErr()) {
   130	        console.error(`  [fail] ${desc.kind.padEnd(16)} — ${formatError(result.error)}`);
   131	        hadError = true;
   132	        continue;
   133	      }
   134	      const run = result.value;
   135	      console.log(
   136	        `  [ ok ] ${desc.kind.padEnd(16)} seen=${String(run.items_seen).padStart(3)} new=${String(run.items_new).padStart(3)} skip=${String(run.items_skipped).padStart(3)}`,
   137	      );
   138	      totalNew += run.items_new;
   139	      totalSkipped += run.items_skipped;
   140	    }
   141	
   142	    console.log(`\ntotal: ${totalNew} new, ${totalSkipped} skipped`);
   143	    return hadError ? 1 : 0;
   144	  } finally {
   145	    runtime.close();
   146	  }
   147	};

/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba src/application/federated-search.ts | sed -n '260,520p'" in /Users/saharbarak/personal/akashik
 succeeded in 0ms:
   260	/**
   261	 * Run a federated search across the local vector index and all connected peers.
   262	 *
   263	 * Steps:
   264	 *   1. Query local vectorIndex (searchByRoom or searchGlobal depending on room param)
   265	 *   2. Parallel fan-out: send SearchRequest to all connected peers via openSearchStream,
   266	 *      each wrapped in withTimeout(2000ms). Uses Promise.all — NOT ResultAsync.combine.
   267	 *   3. Merge local + remote results, dedupe by node_id (prefer local), sort by distance,
   268	 *      slice to top-k. Duplicate peer hits collapsed into _also_from_peers.
   269	 *   4. Tunnel detection: findTunnels over the merged synthetic record set (FED-04).
   270	 *   5. Return FederatedSearchResult with matches, tunnels, and diagnostic counters.
   271	 *
   272	 * When no peers are connected: returns local-only results with peers_queried=0.
   273	 * When a peer errors or times out: its matches are empty; it contributes to
   274	 * peers_timed_out or peers_errored counters.
   275	 */
   276	export const runFederatedSearch = async (
   277	  deps: FederatedSearchDeps,
   278	  params: FederatedSearchParams,
   279	): Promise<FederatedSearchResult> => {
   280	  const k = params.k;
   281	  const room = params.room;
   282	  const perPeerTimeoutMs = params.perPeerTimeoutMs ?? 2000;
   283	  const tunnelThreshold = params.tunnelThreshold ?? 0.6;
   284	
   285	  // Resolve injectable stream opener (test seam)
   286	  const streamOpener = deps.openStream ?? openSearchStream;
   287	
   288	  const t0 = Date.now();
   289	
   290	  // 1. Local query — synchronous from the caller's perspective.
   291	  // Use the hybrid (BM25 + vector + RRF) path whenever the caller supplied
   292	  // the raw text. Hybrid is what the non-federated `searchGlobal` use case
   293	  // calls — without it, federated local-half would silently diverge in
   294	  // retrieval quality from the local-only `ask` path (bug caught while
   295	  // wiring the v2.1 smart-hook to consult peers by default).
   296	  const localRes = params.text
   297	    ? (room
   298	        ? await deps.vectorIndex.searchByRoomHybrid(room as Room, params.text, params.embedding, k)
   299	        : await deps.vectorIndex.searchHybrid(params.text, params.embedding, k))
   300	    : (room
   301	        ? await deps.vectorIndex.searchByRoom(room as Room, params.embedding, k)
   302	        : await deps.vectorIndex.searchGlobal(params.embedding, k));
   303	
   304	  const localMatches: FederatedMatch[] = localRes.isOk()
   305	    ? localRes.value.map((m) => ({
   306	        node_id: m.node_id,
   307	        room: m.room,
   308	        wing: m.wing,
   309	        distance: m.distance,
   310	        _source_peer: null,
   311	      }))
   312	    : [];
   313	
   314	  const t1 = Date.now();
   315	
   316	  // 2a. P2P-scale phase 1 — pubsub fan-out (gossip-first).
   317	  //
   318	  // Replaces N × dialProtocol with one publish + one collector window.
   319	  // Falls through to the legacy per-peer dial path if the gossip
   320	  // collector returns zero responses (no pubsub service, no peers
   321	  // subscribed yet, or floodsub propagation budget exhausted).
   322	  //
   323	  // Tail-aware merge: cap each peer's contribution to ⌈k/divisor⌉
   324	  // so the top-k can't be monopolised by the closest-latency peer
   325	  // returning a stack of near-duplicates. Audit fold-in from
   326	  // .planning/p2p-scale-plan.md Phase 1 mod.
   327	  const gossipDisabled = params.useGossip === false
   328	    || process.env.AKASHIK_SEARCH_GOSSIP === '0';
   329	  let gossipPeerOutcomes: PeerOutcome[] | null = null;
   330	
   331	  if (!gossipDisabled) {
   332	    // Early-exit hint — pass an explicit maxPeers cap when the caller
   333	    // supplied one. Otherwise leave the collector to drain the window:
   334	    // swarm-sim responders can emit many more responses than there
   335	    // are connected libp2p peers, and we don't want to short-circuit
   336	    // those out. Larger swarms set gossipWindowMs to a higher value
   337	    // (e.g. 300ms for a 100-peer swarm).
   338	    const gossipRes = await askGossip(
   339	      deps.node,
   340	      params.embedding,
   341	      room ?? null,
   342	      k,
   343	      {
   344	        // Default 250ms — covers floodsub on a LAN mesh + parallel
   345	        // swarm-responder publish bursts. Tighter latency target?
   346	        // Set params.gossipWindowMs explicitly.
   347	        windowMs: params.gossipWindowMs ?? 250,
   348	        maxPeerResponses: typeof params.maxPeers === 'number' && params.maxPeers > 0
   349	          ? params.maxPeers
   350	          : undefined,
   351	      },
   352	    );
   353	    if (gossipRes.isOk() && gossipRes.value.responses.length > 0) {
   354	      const divisor = params.peerDiversityDivisor ?? 3;
   355	      const perPeerCap = Number.isFinite(divisor) && divisor > 0
   356	        ? Math.max(1, Math.ceil(k / divisor))
   357	        : Number.POSITIVE_INFINITY;
   358	      gossipPeerOutcomes = gossipRes.value.responses.map((r) => ({
   359	        peerId: r.peer_id,
   360	        matches: r.matches.slice(0, perPeerCap) as ReadonlyArray<PeerMatch>,
   361	        status: 'ok' as const,
   362	      }));
   363	    }
   364	  }
   365	
   366	  // 2b. Legacy parallel fan-out — used when gossip is disabled OR
   367	  // returned no responses. NEVER use ResultAsync.combine (short-circuits
   368	  // on first error). Plain Promise.all with per-promise withTimeout.
   369	  const rawPeers = deps.node.getPeers().map((p) => p.toString());
   370	  // Reputation ordering hook — caller may bubble high-rep peers to
   371	  // the front with an epsilon-greedy floor. Defaults to libp2p's
   372	  // native order.
   373	  const orderedPeers = params.peerOrder ? params.peerOrder(rawPeers) : rawPeers;
   374	  // Top-N cap — the actual load-spreading mechanism alongside the
   375	  // rep system's load_factor. After ordering, only the top
   376	  // `maxPeers` get queried; the rest are skipped this round and
   377	  // their rank decays naturally. No cap (default) preserves the
   378	  // current "ask everyone" behaviour.
   379	  const peers = typeof params.maxPeers === 'number' && params.maxPeers > 0
   380	    ? orderedPeers.slice(0, params.maxPeers)
   381	    : orderedPeers;
   382	  const topTierCount = params.topTierCount ?? 3;
   383	  const peerBudgetMs = (idx: number): number =>
   384	    params.lowRankTimeoutMs !== undefined && idx >= topTierCount
   385	      ? params.lowRankTimeoutMs
   386	      : perPeerTimeoutMs;
   387	  const req: SearchRequest = {
   388	    type: 'search',
   389	    embedding: Array.from(params.embedding),  // Float32Array → number[] (JSON-safe, Pitfall 3)
   390	    room,
   391	    k,
   392	  };
   393	
   394	  const peerOutcomes: PeerOutcome[] = gossipPeerOutcomes !== null
   395	    ? gossipPeerOutcomes
   396	    : peers.length === 0
   397	    ? []
   398	    : await Promise.all(
   399	        peers.map((peerId, idx) =>
   400	          withTimeout(
   401	            peerId,
   402	            Promise.resolve(
   403	              streamOpener(deps.node, peerId, req).then(
   404	                (r) => (r.isOk() ? r.value : ([] as ReadonlyArray<PeerMatch>)),
   405	                () => [] as ReadonlyArray<PeerMatch>,
   406	              ),
   407	            ),
   408	            // Tier-aware budget: top-tier peers (idx < topTierCount)
   409	            // get the full perPeerTimeoutMs; tier-2 peers get the
   410	            // tighter `lowRankTimeoutMs` if set.
   411	            peerBudgetMs(idx),
   412	          ),
   413	        ),
   414	      );
   415	
   416	  // peers_queried bookkeeping
   417	  // ─────────────────────────
   418	  // On the gossip path, the asker fans out via a single publish; the
   419	  // responders are whoever subscribes to the request topic and chooses
   420	  // to answer (including swarm-sim virtual peers behind one physical
   421	  // daemon). Reporting peers_queried = peers.length (real libp2p
   422	  // connections) would understate the cooperating set when the
   423	  // responders outnumber the directly-connected peers. Use the
   424	  // outcome count when it exceeds the direct-peer count — that's the
   425	  // honest answer to "how many peers were asked AND chose to answer."
   426	  const peers_queried = gossipPeerOutcomes !== null
   427	    ? Math.max(peers.length, peerOutcomes.length)
   428	    : peers.length;
   429	  const peers_responded = peerOutcomes.filter((o) => o.status === 'ok' && o.matches.length > 0).length;
   430	  const peers_timed_out = peerOutcomes.filter((o) => o.status === 'timeout').length;
   431	  const peers_errored = peerOutcomes.filter((o) => o.status === 'error').length;
   432	
   433	  // Bytes-received estimate: sum of JSON-encoded peer match payloads.
   434	  // PeerMatch is a small object (node_id ≈ 36, room ≈ 12, wing ≈ 12,
   435	  // distance + commas + braces ≈ 30 → ~90 B per row at the 50th
   436	  // percentile). JSON.stringify gives a fair upper bound without
   437	  // forcing the wire layer to expose a byte counter.
   438	  const bytes_received_estimate = peerOutcomes.reduce(
   439	    (acc, o) => acc + (o.matches.length === 0 ? 0 : JSON.stringify(o.matches).length),
   440	    0,
   441	  );
   442	
   443	  const t2 = Date.now();
   444	
   445	  // 3. Merge — dedupe by node_id preferring local, collapse peer dupes into _also_from_peers
   446	  const byId = new Map<string, FederatedMatch>();
   447	  for (const m of localMatches) {
   448	    byId.set(m.node_id, m);
   449	  }
   450	  for (const outcome of peerOutcomes) {
   451	    for (const pm of outcome.matches) {
   452	      const existing = byId.get(pm.node_id);
   453	      if (!existing) {
   454	        byId.set(pm.node_id, {
   455	          node_id: pm.node_id,
   456	          room: pm.room,
   457	          wing: pm.wing,
   458	          distance: pm.distance,
   459	          _source_peer: outcome.peerId,
   460	        });
   461	      } else {
   462	        // Duplicate — prefer the existing entry (local or first-seen peer),
   463	        // append this peer to _also_from_peers (CONTEXT.md discretion decision).
   464	        const already = existing._also_from_peers ?? [];
   465	        byId.set(pm.node_id, {
   466	          ...existing,
   467	          _also_from_peers: [...already, outcome.peerId],
   468	        });
   469	      }
   470	    }
   471	  }
   472	
   473	  const merged = Array.from(byId.values())
   474	    .sort((a, b) => a.distance - b.distance)
   475	    .slice(0, k);
   476	
   477	  // 4. Tunnel detection over the merged synthetic set (FED-04).
   478	  // findTunnelsPure requires VectorRecord[] with `vector` field. We don't have
   479	  // raw vectors from peers — tunnel pass uses local vectors only for merged matches.
   480	  // Peer-only entries are skipped (see computeCrossRoomTunnels comment).
   481	  //
   482	  // Skip path: when params.skipTunnels is set (eg --peers callers), we
   483	  // omit the tunnel pass entirely. Cuts ~150-250ms off the wallclock
   484	  // for federated queries that don't render tunnel output.
   485	  const tunnels = params.skipTunnels
   486	    ? []
   487	    : await computeCrossRoomTunnels(
   488	        deps.vectorIndex,
   489	        merged,
   490	        tunnelThreshold,
   491	      );
   492	
   493	  const t3 = Date.now();
   494	
   495	  return {
   496	    matches: merged,
   497	    tunnels,
   498	    peers_queried,
   499	    peers_responded,
   500	    peers_timed_out,
   501	    peers_errored,
   502	    _telemetry: {
   503	      took_total_ms: t3 - t0,
   504	      took_local_ms: t1 - t0,
   505	      took_fanout_ms: t2 - t1,
   506	      took_merge_ms: t3 - t2,
   507	      bytes_received_estimate,
   508	      // peers_alive — currently == peers_queried because we fan out to
   509	      // every connected peer. When v4.x adds DHT-aware peer selection
   510	      // this will diverge: peers_alive = swarm size, peers_queried = subset.
   511	      peers_alive: peers_queried,
   512	    },
   513	  };
   514	};

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	/**
     2	 * Search sync — one-shot request/response federated search over a libp2p custom protocol.
     3	 *
     4	 * Phase 17 core. Registers /akashik/search/1.0.0 on a libp2p node.
     5	 * Unlike share-sync.ts this is read-only: no Y.js docs, no CRDT mutations,
     6	 * no REMOTE_ORIGIN needed, no debounce timers.
     7	 *
     8	 * CRITICAL invariants — every reviewer must check these:
     9	 *   1. SEARCH_PROTOCOL_ID is '/akashik/search/1.0.0' — separate from
    10	 *      '/akashik/share/1.0.0' so sync and search have independent stream
    11	 *      lifecycles (CONTEXT.md locked decision).
    12	 *
    13	 *   2. FramedStream is COPIED from share-sync.ts, NOT imported. Search and share
    14	 *      streams have independent lifecycles (CONTEXT.md locked decision). If either
    15	 *      protocol evolves its framing, the other should NOT inherit the change
    16	 *      automatically.
    17	 *
    18	 *   3. Float32Array precision (Pitfall 3, 17-RESEARCH.md): outbound embeddings are
    19	 *      sent as JSON number[] via Array.from(embedding). Inbound arrays are
    20	 *      reconstructed via new Float32Array(req.embedding). Precision loss at the
    21	 *      6th–7th decimal place is acceptable for cosine distance comparison.
    22	 *
    23	 *   4. lp.decode yields Uint8ArrayList — frameIter already calls .subarray() to
    24	 *      flatten. Every consumer of frameIter receives a plain Uint8Array (Pitfall 4).
    25	 *
    26	 *   5. Rate limiter Map memory leak (Pitfall 7, 17-RESEARCH.md): evictIdle() is
    27	 *      called on EVERY consume() call. A standalone timer is fragile; inline
    28	 *      pruning ensures eviction runs whenever the bucket is used.
    29	 *
    30	 *   6. Inbound authorization: only rooms listed in local shared-rooms.json respond.
    31	 *      Non-shared rooms return an empty match set with error:'unauthorized'.
    32	 *      No per-peer ACL in Phase 17 — room-level only (CONTEXT.md).
    33	 *
    34	 *   7. Audit log: every inbound search request and outbound response is appended
    35	 *      to share-log.jsonl (same file as share-sync.ts per CONTEXT.md decision).
    36	 */
    37	import * as lp from 'it-length-prefixed';
    38	import { ResultAsync } from 'neverthrow';
    39	import { appendFile, mkdir } from 'node:fs/promises';
    40	import { join, dirname } from 'node:path';
    41	import type { Libp2p, Stream, Connection } from '@libp2p/interface';
    42	import { peerIdFromString } from '@libp2p/peer-id';
    43	import type { SearchError } from '../domain/errors.js';
    44	import { SearchError as SEARCH_ERR } from '../domain/errors.js';
    45	import type { VectorIndex } from './vector-index.js';
    46	import type { Match, Vector } from '../domain/vectors.js';
    47	import { DEFAULT_DIM } from '../domain/vectors.js';
    48	import type { Room } from '../domain/graph.js';
    49	import { loadSharedRooms } from './share-store.js';
    50	
    51	// ─────────────────────── constants ────────────────────────────────────────────
    52	
    53	export const SEARCH_PROTOCOL_ID = '/akashik/search/1.0.0' as const;
    54	const MAX_INBOUND_STREAMS = 32;
    55	/** Per-peer outbound timeout — locked from CONTEXT.md (2s). */
    56	const PER_PEER_TIMEOUT_MS = 2000;
    57	/** Idle eviction threshold — prune peers unseen for 5+ minutes (Pitfall 7). */
    58	const RATE_LIMIT_IDLE_EVICT_MS = 5 * 60 * 1000;
    59	
    60	// ─────────────────────── FramedStream abstraction ─────────────────────────────
    61	
    62	/**
    63	 * Copied from share-sync.ts — NOT imported. Search and share streams have
    64	 * independent lifecycles (CONTEXT.md locked decision). If either protocol
    65	 * evolves its framing, the other should NOT inherit the change automatically.
    66	 *
    67	 * A thin wrapper around a libp2p Stream that encodes/decodes frames with
    68	 * varint length prefixes via it-length-prefixed.
    69	 *
    70	 * write(bytes): length-prefixes and sends via stream.send().
    71	 * The frameIter() generator yields complete decoded frames (as flat
    72	 * Uint8Array) by piping the stream's async iterator through lp.decode().
    73	 */
    74	interface FramedStream {
    75	  /** Send a length-prefixed frame. */
    76	  write(data: Uint8Array): Promise<void>;
    77	  /** Async iterator of decoded frames (Uint8Array, already .subarray()'d). */
    78	  frameIter(): AsyncGenerator<Uint8Array, void, undefined>;
    79	  /** Close the underlying stream. Best-effort. */
    80	  close(): void;
    81	}
    82	
    83	const makeFramedStream = (stream: Stream): FramedStream => {
    84	  const frameIter = async function* (): AsyncGenerator<Uint8Array, void, undefined> {
    85	    // lp.decode(source) accepts AsyncIterable<Uint8Array | Uint8ArrayList>
    86	    // The libp2p Stream IS an AsyncIterable<Uint8Array | Uint8ArrayList>.
    87	    for await (const msg of lp.decode(stream)) {
    88	      // Pitfall 4 — lp.decode yields Uint8ArrayList; .subarray() flattens to Uint8Array.
    89	      yield msg.subarray();
    90	    }
    91	  };
    92	
    93	  return {
    94	    write: async (data: Uint8Array): Promise<void> => {
    95	      // lp.encode([data]) yields one length-prefixed Uint8ArrayList chunk.
    96	      for await (const chunk of lp.encode([data])) {
    97	        stream.send(chunk);
    98	      }
    99	    },
   100	    frameIter,
   101	    close: (): void => { try { void stream.close(); } catch { /* benign */ } },
   102	  };
   103	};
   104	
   105	// ─────────────────────── wire format ──────────────────────────────────────────
   106	
   107	/**
   108	 * Outbound search request.
   109	 * embedding is number[] (JSON-safe) — reconstructed as Float32Array on inbound
   110	 * (Pitfall 3 from 17-RESEARCH.md: acceptable precision loss at 6th-7th decimal).
   111	 */
   112	export interface SearchRequest {
   113	  readonly type: 'search';
   114	  readonly embedding: number[];   // JSON-safe — reconstructed as Float32Array on inbound
   115	  readonly room?: string;
   116	  readonly k: number;
   117	}
   118	
   119	/**
   120	 * A match result annotated with peer provenance.
   121	 * _source_peer: null = local result; string = remote peer PeerId.
   122	 */
   123	export interface PeerMatch extends Match {
   124	  readonly label?: string;         // optional: responding peer includes if available
   125	  readonly source_uri?: string;
   126	  readonly _source_peer: string | null;  // null = local, peerId string = remote
   127	}
   128	
   129	/**
   130	 * Inbound search response from a remote peer.
   131	 * matches omit _source_peer (peer doesn't know its own id).
   132	 * error field is set on failure cases — caller receives empty matches.
   133	 */
   134	export interface SearchResponse {
   135	  readonly type: 'search_response';
   136	  readonly matches: ReadonlyArray<Omit<PeerMatch, '_source_peer'>>;
   137	  readonly error?: 'dimension_mismatch' | 'unauthorized' | 'rate_limited' | 'protocol';
   138	}
   139	
   140	// ─────────────────────── token bucket rate limiter ────────────────────────────
   141	
   142	/**
   143	 * Token bucket state per peer.
   144	 * lastActive is used by evictIdle to prune stale entries (Pitfall 7).
   145	 */
   146	interface BucketState {
   147	  tokens: number;
   148	  lastRefill: number;   // Date.now() ms
   149	  lastActive: number;   // Date.now() ms — for idle eviction (Pitfall 7)
   150	}
   151	
   152	export interface RateLimiter {
   153	  /** Consume one token for peerId. Returns true if allowed, false if rate limited. */
   154	  consume(peerId: string): boolean;
   155	  /**
   156	   * Evict idle buckets. Called internally on each consume (inline pruning).
   157	   * Also exported for tests and admin use.
   158	   * Returns the count of evicted entries.
   159	   */
   160	  evictIdle(nowMs?: number): number;
   161	  /** Test hook — peek current bucket state without mutation. */
   162	  peek(peerId: string): Readonly<BucketState> | undefined;
   163	}
   164	
   165	/**
   166	 * Create a token bucket rate limiter.
   167	 *
   168	 * Tokens refill at ratePerSec tokens/second up to burst capacity.
   169	 * A new peer starts with a full burst bucket.
   170	 *
   171	 * PITFALL LOCK (Pitfall 7): evictIdle() is called inside consume() on every
   172	 * request. A standalone timer is fragile — inline pruning bounds Map growth
   173	 * to active peer count without a separate cleanup lifecycle.
   174	 */
   175	export const createRateLimiter = (
   176	  ratePerSec: number,
   177	  burst: number,
   178	): RateLimiter => {
   179	  const buckets = new Map<string, BucketState>();
   180	
   181	  const evictIdle = (nowMs: number = Date.now()): number => {
   182	    let removed = 0;
   183	    for (const [peer, state] of buckets.entries()) {
   184	      if (nowMs - state.lastActive > RATE_LIMIT_IDLE_EVICT_MS) {
   185	        buckets.delete(peer);
   186	        removed++;
   187	      }
   188	    }
   189	    return removed;
   190	  };
   191	
   192	  const consume = (peerId: string): boolean => {
   193	    const now = Date.now();
   194	    // Cheap incremental prune on every consume call — O(n) but n is
   195	    // bounded by active peer count which is small in practice.
   196	    // Pitfall 7 (17-RESEARCH.md) — unbounded Map growth.
   197	    evictIdle(now);
   198	
   199	    const state = buckets.get(peerId) ?? { tokens: burst, lastRefill: now, lastActive: now };
   200	    const elapsed = (now - state.lastRefill) / 1000;
   201	    const refilled = Math.min(burst, state.tokens + elapsed * ratePerSec);
   202	    if (refilled < 1) {
   203	      buckets.set(peerId, { tokens: refilled, lastRefill: now, lastActive: now });
   204	      return false;   // rate limited
   205	    }
   206	    buckets.set(peerId, { tokens: refilled - 1, lastRefill: now, lastActive: now });
   207	    return true;      // allowed
   208	  };
   209	
   210	  const peek = (peerId: string): Readonly<BucketState> | undefined => buckets.get(peerId);
   211	
   212	  return { consume, evictIdle, peek };
   213	};
   214	
   215	// ─────────────────────── audit log ────────────────────────────────────────────
   216	
   217	/**
   218	 * Search audit log entry. Appended to share-log.jsonl (same file as
   219	 * share-sync.ts per CONTEXT.md decision — single log for all P2P activity).
   220	 */
   221	interface SearchLogEntry {
   222	  readonly timestamp: string;
   223	  readonly peer: string;
   224	  readonly room?: string;
   225	  readonly action: 'search_request' | 'search_response';
   226	  readonly outcome: 'allowed' | 'unauthorized' | 'rate_limited' | 'dimension_mismatch' | 'error';
   227	  readonly k?: number;
   228	  readonly resultCount?: number;
   229	}
   230	
   231	const appendSearchLog = async (logPath: string, entry: SearchLogEntry): Promise<void> => {
   232	  try {
   233	    await mkdir(dirname(logPath), { recursive: true });
   234	    await appendFile(logPath, JSON.stringify(entry) + '\n');
   235	  } catch {
   236	    // best-effort — never fail a request because audit append failed
   237	  }
   238	};
   239	
   240	// ─────────────────────── inbound handler ──────────────────────────────────────
   241	
   242	interface SearchHandlerDeps {
   243	  readonly vectorIndex: VectorIndex;
   244	  readonly sharedRoomsPath: string;
   245	  readonly logPath: string;
   246	  readonly rateLimiter: RateLimiter;
   247	  readonly expectedDim: number;   // DEFAULT_DIM from vectors.ts
   248	}
   249	
   250	/**
   251	 * Handle one inbound search stream.
   252	 *
   253	 * Protocol (one-shot request/response):
   254	 *   1. Read one length-prefixed JSON frame (SearchRequest)
   255	 *   2. Rate-limit check (token bucket per peerId)
   256	 *   3. Dimension guard (req.embedding.length === 384)
   257	 *   4. Room authorization (room must be in local shared-rooms.json)
   258	 *   5. Reconstruct Float32Array from number[] (Pitfall 3)
   259	 *   6. Query vectorIndex.searchByRoom or union-of-shared-rooms global search
   260	 *   7. Write one length-prefixed JSON frame (SearchResponse)

    20	/** A dense unit-normalized float32 vector. */
    21	export type Vector = Float32Array;
    22	
    23	/**
    24	 * A vector in context — carries the id, room, and wing of its node.
    25	 *
    26	 * Optional `raw_text` is the pre-prefix, pre-normalization text used to
    27	 * generate the vector. When present, the vector index can use it to feed
    28	 * a parallel FTS5 BM25 path for hybrid retrieval. When absent, hybrid
    29	 * retrieval gracefully falls back to dense-only.
    30	 */
    31	export interface VectorRecord {
    32	  readonly node_id: NodeId;
    33	  readonly room: Room;
    34	  readonly wing?: Wing;
    35	  readonly vector: Vector;
    36	  readonly raw_text?: string;
    37	}
    38	
    39	/** A similarity match returned by a search. `distance` is L2 on unit vectors. */
    40	export interface Match {
    41	  readonly node_id: NodeId;
    42	  readonly room: Room;
    43	  readonly wing?: Wing;
    44	  readonly distance: number;
    45	}
    46	
    47	/** A pair of nodes from different rooms with a short semantic distance. */
    48	export interface Tunnel {
    49	  readonly a: NodeId;
    50	  readonly b: NodeId;
    51	  readonly room_a: Room;
    52	  readonly room_b: Room;
    53	  readonly distance: number;
    54	}
    55	
    56	// ─────────────────────── validation ───────────────────────
    57	
    58	/** Verify a vector matches the expected dimension. */
    59	export const assertDim = (v: Vector, expected: number = DEFAULT_DIM): Result<Vector, VectorError> =>
    60	  v.length === expected ? ok(v) : err(VectorError.dimensionMismatch(expected, v.length));
    61	
    62	// ─────────────────────── arithmetic ───────────────────────
    63	
    64	/** L2 (Euclidean) distance between two equal-length vectors. */
    65	export const l2 = (a: Vector, b: Vector): number => {
    66	  let s = 0;
    67	  for (let i = 0; i < a.length; i++) {
    68	    const d = a[i] - b[i];
    69	    s += d * d;
    70	  }
   270	  return scored.map((s) => s.candidate);
   271	};
   272	
   273	// ─────────────────────── tunnel detection ────────────────
   274	
   275	/**
   276	 * Find cross-room tunnel candidates — pairs of records from different
   277	 * rooms with L2 distance ≤ threshold.
   278	 *
   279	 * Pure function: no I/O, no mutation. The caller passes a snapshot of
   280	 * all vector records it cares about, and this returns a sorted list.
   281	 *
   282	 * Complexity: O(n²) in the number of records passed. For Phase 1
   283	 * volumes (hundreds) this is fine. Later phases can swap in a
   284	 * nearest-neighbours search driven by the vector index.
   285	 */
   286	export const findTunnels = (
   287	  records: readonly VectorRecord[],
   288	  threshold: number,
   289	  restrictToRoom?: Room,
   290	): readonly Tunnel[] => {
   291	  const tunnels: Tunnel[] = [];
   292	  for (let i = 0; i < records.length; i++) {
   293	    const a = records[i];
   294	    if (restrictToRoom && a.room !== restrictToRoom) continue;
   295	    for (let j = i + 1; j < records.length; j++) {
   296	      const b = records[j];
   297	      if (a.room === b.room) continue; // same room is not a tunnel
   298	      const d = l2(a.vector, b.vector);
   299	      if (d <= threshold) {
   300	        tunnels.push({
   301	          a: a.node_id,
   302	          b: b.node_id,
   303	          room_a: a.room,
   304	          room_b: b.room,
   305	          distance: d,
   306	        });
   307	      }
   308	    }
   309	  }
   310	  return tunnels.sort((x, y) => x.distance - y.distance);
   311	};

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	/**
     2	 * Recency-aware re-ranking for room search.
     3	 *
     4	 * For some rooms (sessions especially) raw cosine distance is the
     5	 * wrong primary signal — a perfect-relevance hit from 18 months ago
     6	 * shouldn't outrank a 0.04-distance-worse hit from yesterday. This
     7	 * module provides a deterministic decay-weighted re-ranker:
     8	 *
     9	 *     relevance = 1 / (1 + distance)               ∈ (0, 1]
    10	 *     decay     = 0.5 ^ (age_days / half_life)     ∈ (0, 1]
    11	 *     combined  = relevance × decay
    12	 *
    13	 * The decay uses an honest half-life: at age == half_life_days the
    14	 * weight is exactly 0.5. (The seductive `exp(-age / half_life)` form
    15	 * gives 0.37 at the half-life, which makes recency policy harder to
    16	 * reason about; `0.5^(age/h)` is the textbook decay.)
    17	 *
    18	 * The room policy maps each known-rerank-eligible room to a
    19	 * half-life (days). Unmapped rooms skip rerank entirely. Read the
    20	 * room from each match (not the query) so a global-search hit set
    21	 * can mix per-room policies.
    22	 *
    23	 * Pure. No I/O. Tested independently of the search layer.
    24	 */
    25	
    26	// ─────────────── room policy ───────────────
    27	
    28	/**
    29	 * Half-life in days, per room. Bigger = more weight on relevance.
    30	 * Smaller = sharper recency preference.
    31	 *
    32	 * - sessions:  30d    — Claude transcripts go stale fast; "what did
    33	 *                       I just figure out?" should usually outrank
    34	 *                       "what did I figure out two months ago?".
    35	 * - research:  14d    — papers / hn / arxiv: fresh signal matters
    36	 *                       more than top relevance after a couple weeks.
    37	 * - toolshed:  60d    — code / deps / git: relevance dominates;
    38	 *                       still some recency lift for live repos.
    39	 *
    40	 * Rooms not listed → no recency rerank.
    41	 */
    42	const HALF_LIFE_DAYS_BY_ROOM: ReadonlyMap<string, number> = new Map([
    43	  ['sessions', 30],
    44	  ['research', 14],
    45	  ['toolshed', 60],
    46	]);
    47	
    48	export const halfLifeForRoom = (room: string | undefined): number | undefined =>
    49	  room === undefined ? undefined : HALF_LIFE_DAYS_BY_ROOM.get(room);
    50	
    51	// ─────────────── rerank ───────────────────
    52	
    53	export interface RankableMatch {
    54	  readonly node_id: string;
    55	  readonly room?: string;
    56	  readonly distance: number;
    57	  /** Days since fetched_at; undefined when the node has no fetched_at. */
    58	  readonly age_days?: number;
    59	}
    60	
    61	const clamp01 = (n: number): number => (n < 0 ? 0 : n > 1 ? 1 : n);
    62	
    63	/**
    64	 * Compute the combined relevance × decay score for a single match.
    65	 * When the room has no recency policy OR the age is unknown, returns
    66	 * relevance only — staying neutral instead of penalising un-aged
    67	 * nodes (those get caught by the satisfaction scorer separately).
    68	 */
    69	export const combinedScore = <M extends RankableMatch>(m: M): number => {
    70	  const relevance = clamp01(1 / (1 + Math.max(0, m.distance)));
    71	  const halfLife = halfLifeForRoom(m.room);
    72	  if (halfLife === undefined || m.age_days === undefined) return relevance;
    73	  const decay = Math.pow(0.5, m.age_days / halfLife);
    74	  return relevance * decay;
    75	};
    76	
    77	/**
    78	 * Re-rank a result set in-place by combined score (relevance × decay).
    79	 * Stable for matches with identical scores. Returns the new ordering.
    80	 *
    81	 * Matches in rooms without a policy keep their relative order under
    82	 * the relevance-only scorer — this means a mixed-room result set
    83	 * gets coherent ranking even when only some rooms have decay.
    84	 */
    85	export const rerankByRecency = <M extends RankableMatch>(
    86	  matches: readonly M[],
    87	): readonly M[] => {
    88	  const scored = matches.map((m, i) => ({ m, i, s: combinedScore(m) }));
    89	  scored.sort((a, b) => {
    90	    if (b.s !== a.s) return b.s - a.s;
    91	    return a.i - b.i; // stable
    92	  });
    93	  return scored.map((x) => x.m);
    94	};
    95	
    96	/**
    97	 * Diagnostic — emit the per-match score breakdown. Used by tests and
    98	 * (eventually) by `akashik ask --explain`.
    99	 */
   100	export interface ScoreBreakdown {
   101	  readonly node_id: string;
   102	  readonly relevance: number;
   103	  readonly decay: number;
   104	  readonly combined: number;
   105	}
   106	
   107	export const explainScore = <M extends RankableMatch>(m: M): ScoreBreakdown => {
   108	  const relevance = clamp01(1 / (1 + Math.max(0, m.distance)));
   109	  const halfLife = halfLifeForRoom(m.room);
   110	  const decay =
   111	    halfLife === undefined || m.age_days === undefined
   112	      ? 1
   113	      : Math.pow(0.5, m.age_days / halfLife);
   114	  return { node_id: m.node_id, relevance, decay, combined: relevance * decay };
   115	};

   250	/**
   251	 * Handle one inbound search stream.
   252	 *
   253	 * Protocol (one-shot request/response):
   254	 *   1. Read one length-prefixed JSON frame (SearchRequest)
   255	 *   2. Rate-limit check (token bucket per peerId)
   256	 *   3. Dimension guard (req.embedding.length === 384)
   257	 *   4. Room authorization (room must be in local shared-rooms.json)
   258	 *   5. Reconstruct Float32Array from number[] (Pitfall 3)
   259	 *   6. Query vectorIndex.searchByRoom or union-of-shared-rooms global search
   260	 *   7. Write one length-prefixed JSON frame (SearchResponse)
   261	 *   8. Append audit log entry
   262	 *   9. Close stream
   263	 */
   264	const handleSearchRequest = async (
   265	  deps: SearchHandlerDeps,
   266	  stream: Stream,
   267	  remotePeerId: string,
   268	): Promise<void> => {
   269	  const fs = makeFramedStream(stream);
   270	  try {
   271	    const iter = fs.frameIter();
   272	    const first = await iter.next();
   273	    if (first.done || !first.value) { fs.close(); return; }
   274	
   275	    // Parse request (JSON over length-prefixed frame)
   276	    let req: SearchRequest;
   277	    try {
   278	      const parsed = JSON.parse(new TextDecoder().decode(first.value)) as unknown;
   279	      if (
   280	        !parsed || typeof parsed !== 'object' ||
   281	        (parsed as { type?: unknown }).type !== 'search' ||
   282	        !Array.isArray((parsed as { embedding?: unknown }).embedding) ||
   283	        typeof (parsed as { k?: unknown }).k !== 'number'
   284	      ) {
   285	        throw new Error('malformed SearchRequest');
   286	      }
   287	      req = parsed as SearchRequest;
   288	    } catch {
   289	      const errResp: SearchResponse = { type: 'search_response', matches: [], error: 'protocol' };
   290	      await fs.write(new TextEncoder().encode(JSON.stringify(errResp)));
   291	      fs.close();
   292	      return;
   293	    }
   294	
   295	    // Rate limit check (Pitfall 7 — evictIdle runs inside consume)
   296	    if (!deps.rateLimiter.consume(remotePeerId)) {
   297	      const errResp: SearchResponse = { type: 'search_response', matches: [], error: 'rate_limited' };
   298	      await fs.write(new TextEncoder().encode(JSON.stringify(errResp)));
   299	      void appendSearchLog(deps.logPath, {
   300	        timestamp: new Date().toISOString(), peer: remotePeerId, room: req.room,
   301	        action: 'search_request', outcome: 'rate_limited', k: req.k,
   302	      });
   303	      fs.close();
   304	      return;
   305	    }
   306	
   307	    // Dimension guard (Pitfall 3 mitigation — check length before Float32Array construction)
   308	    if (req.embedding.length !== deps.expectedDim) {
   309	      const errResp: SearchResponse = { type: 'search_response', matches: [], error: 'dimension_mismatch' };
   310	      await fs.write(new TextEncoder().encode(JSON.stringify(errResp)));
   311	      void appendSearchLog(deps.logPath, {
   312	        timestamp: new Date().toISOString(), peer: remotePeerId, room: req.room,
   313	        action: 'search_request', outcome: 'dimension_mismatch', k: req.k,
   314	      });
   315	      fs.close();
   316	      return;
   317	    }
   318	
   319	    // Room authorization — only respond to rooms listed in shared-rooms.json
   320	    if (req.room !== undefined) {
   321	      const sharedRes = await loadSharedRooms(deps.sharedRoomsPath);
   322	      if (sharedRes.isErr() || !sharedRes.value.rooms.some((r) => r.name === req.room)) {
   323	        const errResp: SearchResponse = { type: 'search_response', matches: [], error: 'unauthorized' };
   324	        await fs.write(new TextEncoder().encode(JSON.stringify(errResp)));
   325	        void appendSearchLog(deps.logPath, {
   326	          timestamp: new Date().toISOString(), peer: remotePeerId, room: req.room,
   327	          action: 'search_request', outcome: 'unauthorized', k: req.k,
   328	        });
   329	        fs.close();
   330	        return;
   331	      }
   332	    }
   333	
   334	    // Log the allowed search_request BEFORE running the query
   335	    void appendSearchLog(deps.logPath, {
   336	      timestamp: new Date().toISOString(), peer: remotePeerId, room: req.room,
   337	      action: 'search_request', outcome: 'allowed', k: req.k,
   338	    });
   339	
   340	    // Reconstruct Float32Array from JSON number[] (Pitfall 3 — acceptable precision loss)
   341	    const embedding: Vector = new Float32Array(req.embedding);
   342	
   343	    // Global queries are ALSO room-restricted: only vectors whose room is in
   344	    // shared-rooms.json may leak out. For safety, an unconstrained global
   345	    // query is converted to the union of shared rooms. If none are shared,
   346	    // return empty matches.
   347	    let matches: readonly Match[] = [];
   348	    if (req.room) {
   349	      const r = await deps.vectorIndex.searchByRoom(req.room as Room, embedding, req.k);
   350	      matches = r.isOk() ? r.value : [];
   351	    } else {
   352	      const sharedRes = await loadSharedRooms(deps.sharedRoomsPath);
   353	      if (sharedRes.isOk() && sharedRes.value.rooms.length > 0) {
   354	        // For each shared room, run searchByRoom and merge. Keep top-k by distance.
   355	        const all: Match[] = [];
   356	        for (const r of sharedRes.value.rooms) {
   357	          const res = await deps.vectorIndex.searchByRoom(r.name as Room, embedding, req.k);
   358	          if (res.isOk()) all.push(...res.value);
   359	        }
   360	        matches = all.sort((a, b) => a.distance - b.distance).slice(0, req.k);
   361	      }
   362	      // else: no shared rooms → empty matches (not an error; normal case for fresh install)
   363	    }
   364	
   365	    const response: SearchResponse = {
   366	      type: 'search_response',
   367	      matches: matches.map((m) => ({
   368	        node_id: m.node_id,
   369	        room: m.room,
   370	        wing: m.wing,
   371	        distance: m.distance,
   372	      })),
   373	    };
   374	    await fs.write(new TextEncoder().encode(JSON.stringify(response)));
   375	    void appendSearchLog(deps.logPath, {
   376	      timestamp: new Date().toISOString(), peer: remotePeerId, room: req.room,
   377	      action: 'search_response', outcome: 'allowed', k: req.k, resultCount: matches.length,
   378	    });
   379	  } finally {
   380	    fs.close();
   381	  }
   382	};
   383	
   384	// ─────────────────────── registry + protocol registration ─────────────────────
   385	
   386	export interface SearchRegistry {
   387	  readonly node: Libp2p;
   388	  readonly deps: SearchHandlerDeps;
   389	}
   390	
   391	/**
   392	 * Create a SearchRegistry. Holds the node reference and all handler deps.
   393	 * Call registerSearchProtocol(registry) to begin accepting inbound search streams.
   394	 */
   395	export const createSearchRegistry = (
   396	  node: Libp2p,
   397	  homePath: string,
   398	  vectorIndex: VectorIndex,
   399	  ratePerSec: number,
   400	  burst: number,
   401	): SearchRegistry => ({
   402	  node,
   403	  deps: {
   404	    vectorIndex,
   405	    sharedRoomsPath: join(homePath, 'shared-rooms.json'),
   406	    logPath: join(homePath, 'share-log.jsonl'),
   407	    rateLimiter: createRateLimiter(ratePerSec, burst),
   408	    expectedDim: DEFAULT_DIM,
   409	  },
   410	});
   411	
   412	/**
   413	 * Register the /akashik/search/1.0.0 protocol on the libp2p node.
   414	 * Idempotent — unhandles any prior registration before re-registering.
   415	 * Mirrors the registerShareProtocol shape from share-sync.ts.
   416	 */
   417	export const registerSearchProtocol = (
   418	  registry: SearchRegistry,
   419	): ResultAsync<void, SearchError> =>
   420	  ResultAsync.fromPromise(
   421	    (async () => {
   422	      // Best-effort cleanup of any prior registration (idempotent).
   423	      try { await registry.node.unhandle(SEARCH_PROTOCOL_ID); } catch { /* benign */ }
   424	      await registry.node.handle(
   425	        SEARCH_PROTOCOL_ID,
   426	        async (stream: Stream, connection: Connection) => {
   427	          const peerIdStr = connection.remotePeer.toString();
   428	          await handleSearchRequest(registry.deps, stream, peerIdStr);
   429	        },
   430	        { runOnLimitedConnection: false, maxInboundStreams: MAX_INBOUND_STREAMS },

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	# P2P Knowledge Graph — Akashik v2.0 Vision
     2	
     3	## The Idea
     4	
     5	Every developer running Akashik has a local knowledge graph. Right now these graphs are isolated — your homelab research doesn't connect to mine.
     6	
     7	**v2.0 makes them connected.** A peer-to-peer network where Akashik nodes discover each other, share graph fragments, and build a collective knowledge layer that's bigger than any single user's research.
     8	
     9	```
    10	Developer A (homelab)          Developer B (ml-papers)
    11	     ┌──────────┐                  ┌──────────┐
    12	     │ 500 nodes│                  │ 800 nodes│
    13	     │ 3 rooms  │                  │ 2 rooms  │
    14	     └────┬─────┘                  └────┬─────┘
    15	          │         P2P mesh             │
    16	          └──────────┬───────────────────┘
    17	                     │
    18	            ┌────────┴────────┐
    19	            │ Shared subgraph │
    20	            │  tunnels across │
    21	            │  both graphs    │
    22	            └─────────────────┘
    23	```
    24	
    25	## How It Works
    26	
    27	### Discovery
    28	- Nodes announce themselves on a local network via mDNS/Bonjour
    29	- Or register with a lightweight coordination server (optional)
    30	- Or manually add peers: `akashik peer add <address>`
    31	
    32	### Sharing Protocol
    33	- Each node exposes a subset of its graph as "public rooms"
    34	- Private rooms stay local (homelab stays mine)
    35	- Shared rooms are replicated via CRDT (conflict-free replicated data types)
    36	- Only node metadata + embeddings are shared — not raw source content
    37	
    38	### Collective Intelligence
    39	- Tunnel detection runs ACROSS peers — my homelab connects to your ml-papers
    40	- Shared discovery loop: if peer B finds a great ArXiv source, peer A gets it suggested
    41	- Federated search: `akashik ask "vector search" --peers` searches across all connected graphs
    42	
    43	### Privacy Model
    44	- **Opt-in only** — nothing shared by default
    45	- **Room-level control** — mark rooms as public/private
    46	- **Metadata only** — share node labels + embeddings, not full text
    47	- **No central server** — peers connect directly
    48	- **Encryption** — all P2P traffic encrypted with peer-to-peer TLS
    49	
    50	## Architecture
    51	
    52	```
    53	src/
    54	  p2p/
    55	    peer.ts              Peer identity (keypair, address, capabilities)
    56	    discovery.ts         mDNS + manual peer registry
    57	    protocol.ts          Graph fragment exchange protocol (protobuf over QUIC)
    58	    replication.ts       CRDT-based room synchronization
    59	    federated-search.ts  Cross-peer semantic search aggregation
    60	    privacy.ts           Room visibility rules, metadata stripping
    61	
    62	  cli/
    63	    commands/
    64	      peer.ts            peer add|remove|list|status
    65	      share.ts           share room|unshare room
    66	```
    67	
    68	### Protocol
    69	
    70	```
    71	PEER_HELLO    → exchange capabilities, room lists, node counts
    72	ROOM_SYNC     → CRDT state vector exchange for shared rooms
    73	NODE_PUSH     → push new nodes (metadata + embedding only)
    74	SEARCH_QUERY  → federated search request
    75	SEARCH_RESULT → aggregated results from peer's local graph
    76	TUNNEL_ALERT  → cross-peer tunnel candidate notification
    77	```
    78	
    79	### CRDT Choice
    80	
    81	Use **Automerge** or **Y.js** for the room-level CRDT:
    82	- Each shared room is a CRDT document
    83	- Node inserts/updates/deletes converge across peers
    84	- No coordination server needed — peers sync directly
    85	- Offline-first — changes queue and sync when reconnected
    86	
    87	## Use Cases
    88	
    89	### Research Teams
    90	A team of 5 researchers each tracks different domains. P2P Akashik connects their graphs. When researcher A indexes a paper about "efficient attention", researcher B (tracking "GPU optimization") gets a tunnel notification: "your GPU optimization connects to A's attention paper."
    91	
    92	### Open Source Communities
    93	A project maintainer shares their `project-x` room publicly. Contributors connect as peers and get the maintainer's research context (relevant papers, HN discussions, competitor analysis) automatically merged into their local graph.
    94	
    95	### Conference Networks
    96	At a conference, attendees run Akashik in P2P mode. Their graphs auto-discover via local network. The collective graph of 100 attendees, each with 500 nodes, creates a 50K-node searchable knowledge base spanning every talk, paper, and conversation.
    97	
    98	## Implementation Phases
    99	
   100	### Phase 15: P2P Foundation
   101	- Peer identity (ed25519 keypair)
   102	- Manual peer management (`peer add/remove/list`)
   103	- Basic graph fragment exchange over WebSocket
   104	
   105	### Phase 16: Room Sharing
   106	- Public/private room marking (`share room/unshare room`)
   107	- CRDT-based room sync (Automerge)
   108	- Metadata-only replication (no raw text)
   109	
   110	### Phase 17: Federated Search
   111	- Cross-peer search aggregation
   112	- Tunnel detection across peers
   113	- Shared discovery loop
   114	
   115	### Phase 18: Production P2P
   116	- mDNS auto-discovery
   117	- QUIC transport (faster than WebSocket)
   118	- Encryption + auth
   119	- Bandwidth management + sync throttling
   120	
   121	## What This Means
   122	
   123	Akashik goes from "your personal research memory" to "a collective intelligence network for developers." Every peer makes the network smarter. The graph grows faster than any individual could build it.
   124	
   125	This is the end state: **a decentralized knowledge graph where every coding agent in the world shares what it learned.**

/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba docs/p2p/peer-reputation-design.md | sed -n '1,220p'" in /Users/saharbarak/personal/akashik
 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	# Peer Reputation System — Design
     2	
     3	**Status:** design ratified by multi-LLM round-4 audit (codex × 2, gemini × 2, claude-sonnet × 2).
     4	Synthesis at `~/.claude-octopus/results/probe-synthesis-1778145513.md` (654 KB).
     5	
     6	**Goal.** Each peer maintains a `peer_id × subject → score` map of every peer
     7	it has ever talked to. After every federated ask, the local satisfaction signal
     8	that the scorer already produces becomes a review of every peer that contributed
     9	to the answer, scoped to the subject(s) the answer was about. Future federated
    10	fan-outs use that map to prefer peers with a track record on the subject —
    11	without ever filtering anyone out, so unknown peers still get sampled.
    12	
    13	---
    14	
    15	## 1. Formal frame — what is this in CS literature?
    16	
    17	A `subject-scoped federated reputation system` over a P2P retrieval network.
    18	Each peer records first-hand post-transaction feedback, aggregates it by
    19	subject, and uses it for peer selection. Six prior works the agents converged on:
    20	
    21	| Work                 | What it got right                                                    | What's wrong-fit for Akashik                                   |
    22	|----------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------|
    23	| **EigenTrust** (Kamvar 2003) | Local feedback + global aggregation; explicit collusion thinking | Single global scalar; "good at libp2p" ≠ "good at lemlist"    |
    24	| **PeerTrust** (Xiong & Liu 2004) | Multi-factor scoring: feedback, volume, source credibility, context | Heavier than v1 needs; overfits sparse data                |
    25	| **PowerTrust** (Zhou & Hwang 2007) | Scalable global aggregation via power nodes              | Soft centralization cuts against room-symmetric peer model     |
    26	| **Beta Reputation** (Jøsang 2002) | Probability + uncertainty (sparse-data handling)         | Wants binary success/failure; we have continuous satisfaction  |
    27	| **Flow-based reputation** (Škorić 2016) | Combines propagation with explicit uncertainty       | Too much machinery for local-only v1                          |
    28	| **SybilGuard** (Yu 2008) | Graph-based identity defense                                   | Assumes social trust graph Akashik doesn't have today         |
    29	
    30	**Local-only vs gossiped:**
    31	- **Local-only** is more defensible (Jøsang's "own experience"). Cold-starts badly.
    32	- **Gossiped** unlocks network memory, faster convergence. Imports collusion,
    33	  replay, Sybil amplification — and our current TOFU DID layer can authenticate
    34	  *who said* something, not whether they were *honest*, *independent*, or *unique*.
    35	
    36	---
    37	
    38	## 2. What ships once this lands
    39	
    40	**Wins:**
    41	- **Smarter fan-out ordering.** Today `federated-search.ts:202` fans out to all
    42	  connected peers in parallel. Reputation lets us sort first, optionally early-stop,
    43	  and budget low-rep peers tighter.
    44	- **"Ask only the experts" mode** for high-stakes queries.
    45	- **Fresh-room bootstrap.** Joining a new room? See who already knows the subject
    46	  and request a connection.
    47	- **Decay/freshness weighting.** A peer who was the lemlist authority 90 days ago
    48	  but stopped reading shouldn't outrank a peer active this week.
    49	- **Audit/provenance.** "This answer came from peers with rep ≥ 0.72 on
    50	  `entity:product:lemlist` and confidence ≥ 0.6." Real SOC2 evidence.
    51	
    52	**Limits — what this does NOT solve:**
    53	- Factual correctness.
    54	- Low-volume subjects (sparse data → low confidence; that's correct, not a flaw).
    55	- Identity uniqueness — TOFU DIDs are still cheap pseudonyms.
    56	- Replacement of the existing satisfaction scorer's provenance/freshness signals.
    57	
    58	**New abuse surface to budget for:** mutual-praise rings, targeted downrating,
    59	topic farming, reviewer capture.
    60	
    61	---
    62	
    63	## 3. Architecture — where it fits
    64	
    65	Reputation is a **sibling subsystem to telemetry**, not a layer inside the existing
    66	satisfaction scorer.
    67	
    68	| Layer | New / extend | Path |
    69	|---|---|---|
    70	| Domain — pure types + math | NEW | `src/domain/peer-reputation.ts` |
    71	| Domain — subject keys | NEW | `src/domain/subject-key.ts` (or fold into peer-reputation.ts) |
    72	| Application — update path | NEW | `src/application/update-peer-reputation.ts` |
    73	| Application — telemetry hook | EXTEND | `src/application/peer-pull-telemetry.ts:40` (emit structured evidence) |
    74	| Application — fan-out ranking | EXTEND | `src/application/federated-search.ts:202` |
    75	| Infrastructure — atomic store | NEW | `src/infrastructure/peer-reputation-store.ts` (patterned after `peer-store.ts:138`) |
    76	| CLI — inspect | NEW | `src/cli/commands/peers-rep.ts` |
    77	
    78	Reuse `computeSatisfaction()` from `peer-telemetry.ts:229` as the per-ask outcome
    79	generator. Don't put the rep math inside the scorer — keep concerns separate.
    80	
    81	**Subject extraction strategy** (rank by stability):
    82	1. **Primary** — canonical `entity_id` from `mentioned_entities` (already on
    83	   AskHit), and from `recall.ts` resolution of the query.
    84	2. **Secondary** — system room key (`room:research`, `room:toolshed`).
    85	3. **Fallback** — embedding-cluster key. Defer to later; volatile, hard to audit.
    86	
    87	**v1 only exposes `entity:*` and `room:*` subject keys.**
    88	
    89	**Aggregation function:** recency-weighted Bayesian mean.
    90	
    91	```
    92	prior_mean         = 0.5
    93	prior_weight (k)   = 3
    94	review_weight (w)  = freshness_decay × provenance_weight × independence_weight
    95	posterior_mean     = (k·0.5 + Σ w_i·score_i) / (k + Σ w_i)
    96	confidence         = Σ w_i / (Σ w_i + k)
    97	rank_score         = posterior_mean × confidence × freshness_multiplier
    98	```
    99	
   100	Defensible because it handles sparse data, supports continuous scores, and
   101	penalises stale/weakly-independent evidence.
   102	
   103	---
   104	
   105	## 4. Data model
   106	
   107	`~/.akashik/peer-reputation.json` — local, atomic temp-write + rename
   108	(mirrors `peer-store.ts:138`):
   109	
   110	```json
   111	{
   112	  "version": 1,
   113	  "local_peer_id": "12D3KooWLocal",
   114	  "updated_at": "2026-05-07T12:00:00.000Z",
   115	  "subjects": {
   116	    "entity:product:lemlist": {
   117	      "label": "lemlist",
   118	      "kind": "entity",
   119	      "peer_scores": {
   120	        "12D3KooWPeerA": {
   121	          "posterior_mean": 0.77,
   122	          "confidence": 0.48,
   123	          "rank_score": 0.34,
   124	          "weighted_review_count": 2.15,
   125	          "raw_review_count": 3,
   126	          "weighted_sum": 1.96,
   127	          "weighted_sum_squares": 1.67,
   128	          "first_review_at": "2026-05-01T09:00:00.000Z",
   129	          "last_review_at": "2026-05-07T10:00:00.000Z",
   130	          "last_answer_at": "2026-05-07T10:00:00.000Z",
   131	          "stale_after_days": 30,
   132	          "decay_half_life_days": 45,
   133	          "reviewers": {
   134	            "did:key:zLocalDid": {
   135	              "weighted_count": 2.15,
   136	              "weighted_sum": 1.96,
   137	              "last_review_at": "2026-05-07T10:00:00.000Z"
   138	            }
   139	          }
   140	        }
   141	      }
   142	    }
   143	  },
   144	  "reviews": [
   145	    {
   146	      "review_id": "rev_001",
   147	      "ask_id": "ask_001",
   148	      "reviewer_did": "did:key:zLocalDid",
   149	      "subject_keys": ["entity:product:lemlist"],
   150	      "target_peer_id": "12D3KooWPeerA",
   151	      "satisfaction_score": 0.92,
   152	      "weight": 1.0,
   153	      "created_at": "2026-05-01T09:00:00.000Z"
   154	    }
   155	  ]
   156	}
   157	```
   158	
   159	**Why two top-level fields?** `subjects` is the materialised aggregation (fast
   160	read for fan-out ranking). `reviews` is the append-only event log (replayable,
   161	audit-friendly, basis for future gossip). Both update atomically together.
   162	
   163	**Worked example after 3 asks with 2 responding peers** (subject `entity:product:lemlist`):
   164	
   165	| Ask | PeerA score | PeerB score |
   166	|-----|-------------|-------------|
   167	| 1   | 0.92        | 0.41        |
   168	| 2   | 0.81        | —           |
   169	| 3   | 0.58        | 0.87        |
   170	
   171	After decay/weights: PeerA accumulates more evidence → moderate posterior_mean,
   172	higher confidence, mid rank_score. PeerB has fewer observations → higher variance
   173	in posterior, lower confidence, lower rank_score even though one of its scores
   174	was higher than any of PeerA's.
   175	
   176	**Edge cases:**
   177	- **Sparse data.** Expose both `posterior_mean` and `confidence`; rank by
   178	  `rank_score`, not raw mean. Beta-style certainty-aware.
   179	- **Conflicting reviews.** Per-reviewer evidence buckets — when gossip lands
   180	  later, aggregate by reviewer DID so one reviewer can't be double-counted
   181	  through multiple relays.
   182	- **Decay.** Apply half-life decay to *evidence*, not just final score —
   183	  naturally lowers both posterior and confidence when a subject goes cold.
   184	
   185	---
   186	
   187	## 5. Wire protocol — three options, ranked
   188	
   189	| Option | Buys | Attack surface | Storage cost | Recommendation |
   190	|---|---|---|---|---|
   191	| **Never propagate (local only)** | Simplest model, no cross-peer attacks, strongest privacy | Local score gaming only | One JSON file | ✅ **Phase 1** |
   192	| **Pull on demand** | Subject-specific bootstrap without continuous gossip ("who knows lemlist around here?") | False summaries, replayed old summaries, Sybil fan-out | Moderate; only hot subjects move | ⏸ **Phase 2** |
   193	| **Gossip via pubsub** | Fastest convergence, passive discovery | Highest. Collusion, replay storms, subject spam, reputation poisoning, storage bloat | Highest | ❌ **Skip until** durable reviewer identity, replay protection, and imported-evidence discount model exist |
   194	
   195	For phase 2 (pull-on-demand), reviews would be wrapped in signed envelopes
   196	analogous to `share-envelope.ts:148` — gives authenticity and timestamp
   197	plausibility, **not truthfulness.**
   198	
   199	---
   200	
   201	## 6. Failure modes + mitigations
   202	
   203	| Failure | Mitigation |
   204	|---|---|
   205	| Cold start, no reviews yet | Prior mean 0.5 + confidence gating; fallback to current all-peer fan-out |
   206	| One bad query distorts profile | Cap per-ask weight contribution; Bayesian prior dilutes outliers |
   207	| Satisfaction-score gaming via verbosity | Rep updates only from normalised scorer output; consider answer-length penalty |
   208	| Mutual-praise ring | Keep first-hand vs second-hand evidence separate; discount imports aggressively |
   209	| Topic atrophy | Half-life decay on evidence; surface `last_answer_at` |
   210	| Subject taxonomy drift | Canonical `entity_id` first; defer embedding-cluster subjects |
   211	| Off-topic vector hits | Require subject-overlap between query-subjects and hit-subjects before crediting |
   212	| Duplicate evidence inflation via `_also_from_peers` | Split or downweight when same node arrived from multiple peers |
   213	| Reviewer identity churn (cheap new DIDs) | Local age/tenure weighting; signed envelopes give continuity, not uniqueness |
   214	| Replay of stale reviews | `review_id`, `signed_at`, monotonic per-reviewer sequence; reject old duplicates |
   215	| Privacy leakage | Subject competence reveals what a peer reads — keep local by default; export with explicit room/subject opt-outs |
   216	| Compliance (B2B / multi-tenant) | Retention policy, deletion semantics, SOC2/GDPR audit trail |
   217	
   218	---
   219	
   220	## 7. Phased implementation — concrete commits

docs/research/beat-the-competitors-retrieval-plan.md:3:**Status:** strategic sketch — Phase 24 candidate ratchets
docs/research/beat-the-competitors-retrieval-plan.md:122:## 6. Stretch — Phase 24 candidates
docs/research/beat-the-competitors-retrieval-plan.md:124:Once leaderboard-leading on retrieval, the next frontier is **hybrid retrieval + LLM extraction with SQuAD-F1 + LLM-judge as competing axes in the composite**. That makes Akashik comparable to mem0's 92.5 LoCoMo composite — different scoring philosophy, head-to-head. Phase 23.8 already laid the SQuAD-F1 foundation; Phase 24 adds the judge axis.
.planning/BENCH-v2.md:123:### 2e. Phase 24-25 — Rust inference sidecar ships, FULL gate passed
.planning/BENCH-v2.md:193:- Lab used the defective-Xenova-bge / correct-Xenova-nomic cached vectors from `sota.db`. Re-running with the Rust-fastembed cache (Phase 24/25) should retain the same shape but shift absolute numbers up across the board.
.planning/v2.1-CANDIDATES.md:20:- **Phase 24** — TDD suite: cover new code paths (hybrid search, BM25 sanitizer, RRF fusion), migration paths (old DB detection, re-index, atomic swap), and a regression bar against Wave 2 numbers (must hit ≥72% NDCG@10 on the bundled SciFact mini-fixture). Update `bench-v2.sh` to use the new in-process pipeline so the bench numbers and the production numbers come from the same code.

.planning/V2.1-SYNTHESIS.md:345:Before kicking off Phase 24, I need your decisions on:
docs/research/octopus-discover/round-5-2026-05-26/README.md:28:> saves locally. Metrics: **`web_fallback_rate(t)`** and
docs/research/octopus-discover/round-5-2026-05-26/README.md:31:> `web_fallback_rate`. Runnable in ~1 week of dev.
docs/research/octopus-discover/round-5-2026-05-26/README.md:54:> - Week 4: publish the `web_fallback_rate` drop
docs/research/octopus-discover/round-5-2026-05-26/README.md:92:> `web_fallback_rate` materially drops over 30 days AND cross-peer
docs/research/octopus-discover/round-5-2026-05-26/README.md:143:- **`web_fallback_rate` telemetry** → extend `src/domain/metrics.ts`
docs/research/octopus-discover/round-5-2026-05-26/README.md:150:  `web_fallback_rate` dropped X% over 30 days across N peers."
docs/research/octopus-discover/round-5-2026-05-26/synthesis.md:94:The next engineering month must be spent on **architecting the federation and its measurement infrastructure (AkashikBench-F)**. The empirical data confirms the LME-S head is saturated (R@50=1.000, ~4pp NDCG headroom). Chasing the final 2-3pp to match `agentmemory`'s 0.952 is a vanity metric for a single-user memory product, which Akashik is no longer. The core differentiator is network compounding. Conversely, the tradeoff of abandoning single-peer tuning is leaving 60pp of factual headroom on LoCoMo unmined, meaning local retrieval may remain noisy. However, building the federation layer (CRDT sync, query fan-out) and measuring the `web_fallback_rate` is the only way to empirically validate the actual "knowledge commons" thesis.
docs/research/octopus-discover/round-5-2026-05-26/synthesis.md:102:*   **Metrics:** `web_fallback_rate(t)` and `Propagation Half-Life` (median time until a newly acquired fact reaches 50% peer coverage). Compounding is defined quantitatively as the negative slope of the `web_fallback_rate` over the simulation.
docs/research/octopus-discover/round-5-2026-05-26/synthesis.md:113:*   **Timeline:** Week 1: 5-10 "librarian" maintainers seed the graph. Week 2: Onboard 80-90 early adopters. Week 3: Run organic debugging queries. Week 4: Publish the `web_fallback_rate` drop.
docs/research/octopus-discover/round-5-2026-05-26/synthesis.md:129:*   **Response:** That critique is devastatingly valid unless we can demonstrate a dense room where the `web_fallback_rate` materially drops over 30 days and cross-peer transfers happen faster than fresh web research. Our architecture mitigates the cold-start problem because the local-first web-fallback loop is highly useful on day 1 for the individual. The tradeoff is brutal, however: if the first pilot fails to show repeated miss-to-hit conversions across different users under real churn, the federated-commons thesis is empirically disproven, and the product devolves into local-first memory sync.
docs/research/octopus-discover/round-5-2026-05-26/synthesis.md:149:| **`web_fallback_rate` Telemetry Pipeline** | High | Low | **High** |
.planning/debates/rooms-abstraction-2026-05-26/round1-codex-positionA.md:60:## Federation health context (Phase 24 in PROJECT-PLAN-AKASHIK.md)
